manualEntries.entryContent:
**Follow the steps below to create a Template dynamically.** **Step 1: **Visit the Templating module on Jquiver home. **Step 2 : **Click on Add Template, it will open up a form to add/edit Template. ![](/cf/files/b0bfdd7e-3c31-4974-b22f-e103ab533d19) **Step 3 : ** Give any Template name and choose the type of Template you want to create from the Default Template dropdown according to your requirement or you can simply write your code in the HTML editor. ![](/cf/files/ebaf8c2a-3932-48be-9418-616f94f5cebd) **Step 4 : ** After entering the required details, save the Template. ![](/cf/files/aa882b04-dc5e-4ec3-a299-e3409195fff3) ![](/cf/files/105451da-4d8c-4c3f-aab7-602a356c8613) **Step 5 : **After saving, the template will appear in the listing page as shown. ![](/cf/files/5d29a3c4-7013-4cab-bb59-ebb9af9321ec) **Step 6 : **We can edit the Template in the Edit mode. ![](/cf/files/ef715386-7f92-40a7-b950-c5a169bf3055) **How To Embed a Template:** We can include a template within another template by using templateWithoutParams {}, templateWithParams {} **Code Snippet:** <@templateWithoutParams "role-autocomplete" />
<#assign ufAttributes = { "entityType": "Templating", "entityId": "templateId", "entityName": "vmName", "hideFav":"true" }> <@templateWithParams "user-favorite-template" ufAttributes />

Form IO is a powerful form and data management platform that enables developers to build, deploy, and manage dynamic forms and APIs quickly, using a drag-and-drop interface or JSON configurations. It is widely used in modern web applications for form creation, workflow management, and backend integration.
<u>**Steps to create Form IO:**</u>
**Step 1:** Navigate to the Form IO Module from the home page.
**Step 2:**Click on Create New Button.
![](/cf/files/5c21c9f3-9345-45cf-9075-d56c1d7b8e5a)
**Step 3:**Form IO page will be opened where the user can enter the form name,description etc.
![](/cf/files/7ed78fef-3b72-44e6-8cbd-82bd35d01133)
**Step 4:** There are 4 form components panel present in the left pane.
| Panel | Examples | | -------------- | -------------------------------------- | | Basic | Text Field, Text Area, Number, Password, CheckBox, Select Boxes, Select, Radio, Button | | Advanced | Email, Url, Phone, Tags, Address, Date/Time, Day, Time, Currency, Survey, Signature | | Layout | HTML Element, Content,Columns, Field Set, Panel,Table, Tabs, Well | | Data | Hidden, Container, Data Map, Data Grid, Edit Grid |
🔹<u>** Tabs for Each Component:**</u>
![](/cf/files/a4c5fb50-9508-4d46-ac49-8570b35aa805)
<u>**General Settings**</u>
**Below is a list of general settings that are offered for the majority of the Form IO components.**
**Label - **The name or title for this component **Hide Label - **Hide the label of this component. This setting will display the label in the form builder, but hide the label when the form is rendered. **Label Position - **Position for the label for this field. **Label Width - **The width of label on line in percentages. **Label Margin - **The width of label margin on line in percentages. **Placeholder - **The placeholder text that will appear when this field is empty. **Description - **The Description is text that will appear below the input field. **Tooltip - **Adds a tooltip icon to the side of this field. **Error Label - **The label that will display for the field when a validation error message is shown. Input Mask -An input mask helps the user with input by ensuring a predefined format. For a phone number field, the input mask defaults to (999) 999-9999. Allow Multiple masks -This setting will allow you to set multiple input masks for the field. The mask is selected by the user via a dropdown list and will dynamically switch the mask for the field when selected. This feature is only availabe on our formio.js renderer. **Prefix - **The text to show before a field. An example is ‘$’ for money **Suffix - **The text to show after a field. An example would be ‘lbs’ for weight. **Custom CSS Class - **A custom CSS class to add to this component. You may add multiple class names separated by a space. **Tab Index - **Sets the tabindex attribute of this component to override the tab order of the form. See the MDN documentation on tabindex for more information on how it works. **Multiple Values - **If checked, multiple values can be added in this field. The values will appear as an array in the API and an “Add Another” button will be visible on the field allowing the creation of additional fields for this component. **Enable Spell Check - **This setting will enable spell check on the field. **Protected - **If checked, this field is for input only. When being queried by the API it will not appear in the properties. You can still see the value on form.io by viewing the form submissions. **Persistent - **If checked, the field will be stored in the database. If you want a field to not save, uncheck this box. This is useful for fields like password validation that shouldn’t save. **Encrypted - **Encrypt this field on the server. This is two way encryption which is not be suitable for passwords. This setting is only available on the ‘Enterprise’ project plan. **Hidden - **A hidden field is still a part of the form JSON, but is hidden when viewing the form is rendererd. Initial Focus Make this field the initially focused element on this form when renderered. Only one component on this form or wizard page can carry the Initial Focus setting. **Hide Input - **Hide the input when viewing the form from the front end browser. This does not encrypt on the server. Do not use for passwords. **Disabled - **Disable this field on the form. **Show Label in DataGrid- **Show the label of this component when in a datagrid. **Table View - **If checked, this value will show up in the table view of the submissions list.
<u>**Steps to create a form using FormIO:**</u>
**Step1:** Drag and drop the components matching the columns of the custom table created in DB.
**Step2:** Suppose if you want to include FirstName, LastName and Age, then first you have to enter a hidden component for primary key id. Then drag text fields for FirstName and LastName and Number component for Age.
**Note:** Primary Key field can be kept hidden or visible based on the user's requirement.
**Step3**:After addind a form Component, if the user wants to edit a Form Component, then he/she has to hover over the component and click the gear icon. Then a settings form for the component would be opened. Refer the image below.
![](/cf/files/69464142-2c80-4635-995b-44bdd5b28019)
**Note:** To achieve multilingual functionality of Jquiver in Form IO just type ${jws.firstName} in Label under Display Tab. Then the text for First Name will be displayed which is handled in our Internationalization module.
**Refer the image below:**
![](/cf/files/d9c1dcf7-586b-4416-a44c-a28d4c62dc08)
**Step3**: Under the Data Tab if the user wants to give any default value then those details can be given.
![](/cf/files/0c7d2297-59a8-4a1c-9aaf-34d863e981ec)
**Step 4:** Under the validation tab, the user can input necessary validations like mandatory, minimum and maximum length etc depending on the type of components chosen.
![](/cf/files/98ab1990-477c-4fa7-ac43-609275fcbf4f)
**Step 5:** Under the API tab, in property name the user needs to map the input field to the column present in the DB table.
![](/cf/files/a6673d2e-6482-43b0-bcad-40a3ba7e71d1)
**Step 6:** The Conditional tab allows you to show or hide a component dynamically based on the value of another form field.
**Example:** Suppose there are two fields one drop-down (userType) and another one text field(studentId). The drop-down has two values as student and teacher. The text field(studentId) should only be visible when userType == "student".
In this case ,the Conditional Tab of studentId should be configured as follows:
| **Field** | **Value** | | ----------------------------- | ------------ | | This component should Display | **True** | | When the form component | **userType** | | Has the value | **student** |
In the image given below, the userType value is teacher, so the Student Id text field is not displayed.
![](/cf/files/0432bb11-2c63-4351-b9a1-50648fd80b09)
Whereas in the image below when userType equals to student, then only the Student Id text field is displayed.
![](/cf/files/23df4387-961c-49d3-bda5-0324803b0db2)
**Note:** For components like Survey and Signature , the user needs to handle the logic for displaying the values in the edit mode.
<u>**Example of some complex components under Layout Tab:**</u>
**Steps to add HTML Element:**
* Drag and drop the HTML Element from Advanced components.
* In the Content field, add your desired HTML. Refer the images below:
![](/cf/files/246a7e5a-f956-43ee-a933-bf3bcd937717)
![](/cf/files/66a85a0d-a339-48e4-a85b-1a1c03351225)
* Save and preview the form.
![](/cf/files/62113bc5-a4c5-422e-b07a-118b812e7fad)
**Steps to add Content Component:**
The content element under Layout tab can be used in same way as HTML element by following the steps:
* Go to the Layout tab in Form IO .
* Drag and drop the Content component.
* In the Content field, insert your content.Refer the image below.
![](/cf/files/54725275-0a10-437d-a7f7-01f38cc2fae1)
* Preview the form and save.Refer the image below.
![](/cf/files/7ea4157f-9792-4751-8789-230dd042ce54)
* Content will be displayed as shown below in the image:
![](/cf/files/e2cb93f3-4659-423b-841d-4a879fd764b8)
**Steps to add Columns Component:**
The Columns component under Layout tab can be used by following the steps:
* Go to the Layout tab in Form IO .
* Drag and drop the Columns component.
* By default, it gives you 2 columns. You can adjust the width (% or Bootstrap grid units).The user can add more columns by clicking on the Add option.
* Inside each column, the fields can be dragged.Refer the image below.
![](/cf/files/7d8bd18b-03a6-438e-8553-39975449152c)
* Preview the form and save.
* Content will be displayed as shown below in the image:
![](/cf/files/877b7ba3-4dbe-4b4a-afef-fc24f3ccd2b6)
**Steps to add Field Set Component :**
The Field Set Component under Layout tab is used to group related form fields under a common legend/title, much like an HTML tag. It's useful for logically separating sections like "Personal Info", "Address", or "Emergency Contact". by following the steps:
* Go to the Layout tab in Form IO .
* Drag and drop the Field Set component.
* Set the Legend (e.g., "Personal Information").Refer the image below.
![](/cf/files/69093350-b9f5-4eeb-b6af-463d8134bff2)
* Inside the Field Set, drag and drop:
* A Text Field labeled First Name
* A Text Field labeled Last Name
* An Email Field labeled Email
Refer the image below.
![](/cf/files/17e844e3-66b7-4fa3-83fc-361dab4fe0f6)
* Preview the form and save.
* Field Set will be displayed as shown below in the image:
![](/cf/files/f763d95a-2f36-4c7d-a3d2-7f08613265e1)
**Steps to add Panel Component :**
The Panel component is used to group related fields inside a titled and optionally collapsible container. This improves the visual structure and user experience.
* Go to the Layout tab in Form IO .
* Drag and drop the Panel component.
* Click on the Panel component you just added.
* In the Display tab on the right:
* Title: Set a meaningful title (e.g., Personal Details).
Refer the image below.
![](/cf/files/f24d9242-063e-4923-bbdf-45f75bf80cfe)
* Inside the Panel, drag and drop:
* A Text Field labeled First Name
* A Text Field labeled Last Name
* An Email Field labeled Email
Refer the image below.
![](/cf/files/9e785abd-7803-4f82-b434-96b2a4eeba2b)
* Preview the form and save.
* Panel will be displayed as shown below in the image:
![](/cf/files/edc99cdd-7787-44d1-8116-188c7e3e1310)
**Note:** Similarly all other components under the layout tab can be added in Form IO.
After saving the Form IO page, there are two ways to use FormIO in Jquiver Application.
<u>**Steps to follow after saving the Form IO with the desired components:**</u>
* Go to Form Builder and create a new Form and enable Form IO Toggle Button, then select the Form IO that had been created from the Form IO auto complete and choose the table then automatically the Select Script, HTML and Save Query Editors contents would be populated.After that create the Router for Form Builder. After that the Form IO is accessed through the Router menu.
**HTML Editor Content Explanation:** This script dynamically renders and manages a form using Form.io, binds event listeners for form submission and validation, manages hidden field handling for checkboxes, handles CAPTCHA image loading, and defines success/error handlers for form submission.
🔧 **1. Form Configuration & Initialization**
```let formMetaData = ${(formMetaData)!""}; let formOptions = { disableAlerts: true, noAlerts: true };```
* formMetaData contains the JSON schema used to render the dynamic form using Form.io.
* formOptions disables alerts from Form.io (alerts will be handled manually or using a custom function).
🧩 **2. Rendering the Form**
```Formio.createForm(document.getElementById('formioBody'), formMetaData, formOptions)```
* This renders the Form.io form inside the <div id="formioBody">.
* After rendering, default data (form.submission) is set using resultSetObject.formData.
📥 **3. Handling Form Submission**
```form.on('submit', function(submission) { submission.data.id = $("#primaryKeyId").val(); ... onSaveButtonClick(isEdit); });```
**On form submission:**
* The primaryKeyId is added to submission data.
* If the primary key column (primaryKeyColumnName) is present, it's updated into the hidden field.
* Then onSaveButtonClick() is triggered to save the form data.
❌ **4. Cancel Event (Optional Use)**
**Note:** If the user wants to add any more buttons apart from the predefined ones. Then the button can be added as shown:
![](/cf/files/2d57e3fc-8c58-4e2a-a006-5ac7b2fb70d4)
**Java Script Event Binding example for the button:**
🔸 **Example: On Button Click**
``` form.on('cancelFormIoEvent', function(submission) { /* Your custom logic for canceling the form.You can redirect or clear the form fields*/ form.reset(); // Reset the form fields window.location.href = '/home'; // Optionally redirect to another page });```
* Reserved for cancel event logic (e.g., reset form or redirect).
* Currently includes commented-out logic you can customize.
✅ **5. Checkbox Handling**
```$('.form-check-input').change(function() { ... });```
* Ensures that unchecked checkboxes still submit a value (e.g., 0).
* If a checkbox is unchecked, it inserts a hidden field with value=0.
📋 **6. Client-Side Validation**
```function onValidation(a_serializedFormData) { ... }```
* Validates each form field based on regex patterns defined in fieldList.
* Highlights invalid fields and displays warning messages using showMessage().
* Prevents submission if validation fails.
🔐 **7. CAPTCHA Management**
**a. Load CAPTCHA**
```function generateCaptch(imagesrc) { ... }```
* Fetches CAPTCHA image via API (imagesrc URL).
* Updates the image and handles errors.
* Stores and updates request header (r) for session management.
**b. Reload CAPTCHA**
```function reloadCptcha() { ... }```
* Constructs the CAPTCHA image path and reloads it.
**c. Reload Button Listener**
```$(document).on("click", "#reloadCaptcha", function(event) { ... });```
* Triggers reload when the CAPTCHA refresh button is clicked.
✅ **8. Response Handlers**
**a. Success**
```function onSuccess(a_serverResponse){ ... }```
* Redirects to the module listing page upon successful save.
**b. Error**
```function onError(message, exception){ ... }```
* Displays error message on failure using showMessage().
📌 **Integration Points:**
* **${contextPath}:** Application base path for generating resource URLs.
* **${formMetaData}:** JSON structure for Form.io to generate fields.
* **${fieldList}:** List of field configurations including regex for validation.
* **${primaryKeyColumnName}:** Dynamic key used to update hidden field for record updates.
💡 **Notes for Developers:**
* Update primaryKeyColumnName and moduleURL as per your database schema.
* Ensure that formMetaData, fieldList, and other variables are set server-side or in FTL.
* To handle submission manually or through APIs, customize onSaveButtonClick() accordingly.
* Select Script Editor and Save Script Editor contents can be customizable as per the user's requirements.
<u>**Other ways to create Form IO:**</u>
Navigate to Master Generator from home page and enable the Form IO option and select the custom table and fill out the other details as is done while creating a form using Master Generator. Here form, listing page and Router menu along with grid details are created . Refer the image below.
![](/cf/files/7558a889-4815-40f9-a76c-b114fd0bcd86)
Captcha can also be enabled in Form IO using Master Generator as shown in the image below:
![](/cf/files/91cfc267-638d-4955-955b-119b54ca2e2c)
** Note: ** By default the Persistent Type dropdown value will be default as shown in the image below.
![](/cf/files/a83993b1-0c9c-4ed2-a38f-72400d1d0070)
** Default:** Here the normal steps to create FormIO are followed like creating formIO manually and then creating Form Builder.
![](/cf/files/d68b9dd5-5dfb-4cc6-8b00-e80662d6c187)
** Custom:** The custom option is provided to be used mainly by the end users who are unaware of the internal flow of the application. Here only the desired fields needed for the form to be dragged and dropped and the rest of the flow is handled internally. This option will load the data required for the page using the custom rest API "fetchForm". This method is used to get information about data required while pre- populating page in edit mode. The request params required for the Rest API are 1. formIoId : the primary key value of the form IO used. 2. dataId : Primary key value required to get the information about the data stored in the data table. The response of this rest api should be formData as a json format.
For saving the data of the form into the database, another custom RestAPI is called which is "saveForm". If saveForm is used, the request params required for the rest api are : 1. isEdit : For new records the value will be 0. For existing records it will be 1. 2. formIoId : the primary key value required to render the page. 3. dataId : Primary key value required to get the information about the data stored in the table. 4. formData: This variable is availble from Form IO Framework as submission variable after submitting the form.

Versioning plays pivotal role when developing an application which require frequent changes and keeping track of previous version is very important.
<u>**Steps to save the versioning details :**</u>
**Step 1: ** Click on revision button from the listing page: ![](/cf/files/c1052fca-406c-473d-8d26-19a6cb1641e1)
**Step 2: ** Select revision time using autocomplete: ![](/cf/files/20407136-778d-4237-a355-dcdbefc74703)
**Step 3: ** See the difference between two versions ![](/cf/files/a15256c3-7bec-4577-9c15-f9d8668ca68f) ![](/cf/files/915c16d4-7de6-4026-87ee-4ab39b2b4c19)
**Step 4: ** If you want you can replace current data with old data using copy button and then save the changes. ![](/cf/files/aa99668a-7aba-4d09-8c04-af127289db1f) ![](/cf/files/8961a88b-be30-452e-9ae7-314edf76e340)
**Complex example:**
![](/cf/files/1e44e825-7d8d-4485-8e59-20a74888106b)
![](/cf/files/1c012d8f-750d-4af1-aa07-0dab31cd50ac)
![](/cf/files/0c402fca-cf3a-41dd-8693-56156e5cab67)

Help Manual module helps us to understand the application. In this module we can store all the documentation related to the application, as per users need. By default we have provided, JQuiver Help Manual which helps you to understand the JQuiver application, its features and functionality support and how to use JQuiver to build your own application.
**To create/view a help manual, go to Help Manual option from the main page.** We support custom and system help manuals. Jquiver Help Manual is a system help manual, which explains you about the JQuiver application. All other user created help manuals are custom type manuals. ![](/cf/files/60c87dcf-ef2b-4b09-a78a-84ddba2ddb65)
To create a new manual, click on "Create New Manual" button. This will help you to create a new custom manual. Every manual has role based access. As per the role assigned, manuals can be viewed and edited. If a manual has no "Authenticated" permission, then it will not be available for Authenticated users. ![](/cf/files/361d58dd-7e08-43f2-99a2-d04ccc8f058d) You can add any kind of css-style-classes to your help manual. After creating click on the save button. ![](/cf/files/0521704b-b4a2-476b-b32f-3ae8b41bc654)
Once a manual is created, it will be displayed in the listing page as shown. ![](/cf/files/160541df-ac77-4eff-81a2-45182b314fab)
By clicking on the edit button, you will be able to add entries and content in your help manual. ![](/cf/files/5ca28000-972c-4836-be11-015f64897ce5)
The following page will be opened, and by clicking the Create 1st Level Topic, the first node/entry will be created in the left section as shown in the image. You can type any name for the entries. ![](/cf/files/870b3b36-f04b-4ca7-ba3a-8d96ad543414)
After creating the first node, you can enter the content in the right hand section of the editor as shown in the image. ![](/cf/files/679b58b9-6e18-4fe6-b5d8-94355b87d619)
You can create child nodes from the parent node by right clicking on it and then clicking on Create. ![](/cf/files/4580be2b-2d74-416a-b017-f42ad47c56fe)
Then you can give any name to the child node and also write content about it in the editor. ![](/cf/files/3f4f5632-4edb-4805-bea9-172e1bcb1a8b)
There is provision for including files for every parent/child nodes. The number of files to be uploaded in the entry depends upon the configured file count in file bin. ![](/cf/files/b6d1e19c-8580-4d2f-8345-29add70116df)
**Note:-** No file will get uploaded unless the manual entry is already available in the DB. Make sure you save the manual entry first without any file and then edit it to upload the files/images.
Once the manual entries are made, using "View Manual", you can view the manual as below. ![](/cf/files/55dd74f7-7191-4704-98a0-5062a40d8e8a)
Your manual can be viewed either from "Help Manual" module, or you can provide an entry in Router to access your manual from menu or any other location in your application.

**How can I use Grid Utils to manage data grids?**
Use Grid Utils to efficiently manage and interact with tabular data within the application. It provides built-in functionalities such as sorting, filtering, pagination, and data manipulation, allowing you to handle large datasets with ease.
You can configure grid behavior, customize columns, and apply actions like edit, delete, or view directly from the grid interface. Grid Utils also supports dynamic data binding, making it easier to integrate with APIs and backend services.
By using Grid Utils, you can enhance data visibility, improve user interaction, and streamline operations on structured data. <u>**Steps to create grid details.**</u>
**Step 1: -** Navigate to Grid Utils module from home page.
**Step 2 :- **Add a new entry. ![](/cf/files/16afb46c-8249-4e46-975e-ad27250792f1)
**Step 3 :- **Fill in the required details . * Enter the Grid Id, Grid Name and Grid Description. * Grid Table name can have table name, view name and Stored Procedure name as a value. * Grid Column Names are comma separated values of the table columns or simply you can add *. * Query Type dropdown will have view/Table or Stored Procedure option. * You can add any filter criteria for pq-grid in Additional Filter Criteria text box like file_path <> "/images" etc.
![](/cf/files/18d495df-c22f-4976-8b7d-82a114e650e9)
**Step 4 :- **After creating the grid entry, go to templates modules from Jquiver home page and click on add template, a form will get opened, enter the template name in which the grid will be displayed and select the default-template-listing from Default Template drop-down, on selecting it will create the basic template for your grid listing page. ![](/cf/files/1fb8573a-5272-450e-9e1a-a88337c8c5a4)
**Step 5 :- **Modify the col models accordingly in template, and pass the appropriate values in the grid initialization.
**Configure Conditional Expressions :** Grid Utils provides the capability to configure conditional expressions, which helps users filter and display records based on specific conditions applied to grid columns. By using conditional operators, users can perform accurate data filtering for numeric values, text values, dates, and other column types within the grid. This feature improves data analysis and allows users to quickly identify the required records from large datasets.
Supported conditional operators include: * Equal To (eq). * Not Equal To (neq). * Greater Than (gt). * Less Than (lt). * Greater Than or Equal To (gte). * Less Than or Equal To (lte). * Between (btw).
**Code snippet :**
let postData = { gridId: "grid-id"};
Creates a postData object with the grid identifier. This object is used to store filter parameters and send them dynamically while loading grid data.
* Ex 1: postData.cr_table_name = "str_" + val; postData.cmp_table_name = "eq";
* Ex 2: postData.cr_table_name = "str_" + min + "," + max; postData.cmp_table_name = "btw"; postData.type_table_name = "int";
Configures the filter criteria, comparison operator, and logical operator for the grid filtering process. * cr_table_name is used to pass dynamic filter or range values. * cmp_table_name defines the comparison condition such as eq, gt,btw etc. * type_table_name = "int" specifies the data type of the filter values as integer.
let gridNew = $("#grid-id").pqGrid(); gridNew.pqGrid("option", "dataModel.postData", postData); gridNew.pqGrid("refreshDataAndView");
Retrieves the pqGrid instance, updates the postData configuration with the applied filter parameters, and refreshes the grid data and view dynamically based on the configured conditions.
**Apply Pattern Matching Filters:** Grid Utils supports advanced pattern matching filters that allow users to search records based on partial text values instead of exact matches. This functionality is useful when users need to search records using incomplete text, prefixes, suffixes, or matching keywords. Pattern matching filters improve the overall search experience and make it easier to locate relevant information within large datasets.
Supported pattern matching operators include: * Contains (contain) * Does Not Contain (dnc) * Starts With (sw) * Ends With (ew)
**Code snippet :**
let postData = { gridId: "grid-id"};
Creates a postData object with the grid identifier. This object is used to store filter parameters and send them dynamically while loading grid data.
postData.cr_table_name = "str_" + val; postData.cmp_table_name = "contain";
Configures the filter criteria, comparison operator, and logical operator for the grid filtering process. * cr_table_name is used to pass dynamic filter or range values. * cmp_table_name defines the comparison condition such as contain,dnc, sw,ew etc.
let gridNew = $("#grid-id").pqGrid(); gridNew.pqGrid("option", "dataModel.postData", postData); gridNew.pqGrid("refreshDataAndView");
Retrieves the pqGrid instance, updates the postData configuration with the applied filter parameters, and refreshes the grid data and view dynamically based on the configured conditions.
**Build Logical Query Flows (AND / OR):** Grid Utils provides support for building logical query flows by combining multiple filter conditions using logical operators such as AND and OR. This functionality enables users to create complex filtering logic and retrieve highly specific data from the grid based on multiple criteria.
Grid Utils allows combining multiple filter conditions using logical operators such as AND and OR.
**Code snippet :**
let postData = { gridId: "grid-id"};
Creates a postData object with the grid identifier. This object is used to store filter parameters and send them dynamically while loading grid data.
postData.cr_table_name = "str_" + min + "," + max; postData.cmp_table_name = "contain"; postData.lo_table_name = "OR";
Configures the filter criteria, comparison operator, and logical operator for the grid filtering process.
* cr_table_name is used to pass dynamic filter or range values. * cmp_table_name defines the comparison condition such as contain, eq, gt, etc. * lo_table_name defines the logical query flow using operators such as AND or OR.
let gridNew = $("#grid-id").pqGrid(); gridNew.pqGrid("option", "dataModel.postData", postData); gridNew.pqGrid("refreshDataAndView");
Retrieves the pqGrid instance, updates the postData configuration with the applied filter parameters, and refreshes the grid data and view dynamically based on the configured conditions.
**Supported Filter Operators in Grid Utils:** Grid Utils supports the following conditional and pattern matching operators for dynamic filtering.
| Operator | Description | Usage | SQL Equivalent | |---|---|---|---| | `eq` | The `eq` operator is used to filter records where the column value exactly matches the specified value entered by the user. This operator is commonly used for exact value comparisons in grids. | `status = ACTIVE` | `=` | | `neq` | The `neq` operator is used to filter records where the column value does not match the specified value. This helps exclude unwanted records from the grid result. | `status != INACTIVE` | `!=` | | `gt` | The `gt` operator is used to display records where the column value is greater than the specified value. It is generally used for numeric and date comparisons. | `salary > 50000` | `>` | | `lt` | The `lt` operator is used to display records where the column value is less than the specified value. This is useful for filtering ranges and thresholds. | `salary < 50000` | `<` | | `gte` | The `gte` operator is used to filter records where the column value is greater than or equal to the specified value entered by the user. | `salary >= 50000` | `>=` | | `lte` | The `lte` operator is used to filter records where the column value is less than or equal to the specified value. | `salary <= 50000` | `<=` | | `contain` | The `contain` operator is used for pattern matching and returns records where the specified text is available anywhere within the column value. This improves partial text searching within grids. | `employee_name contains 'John'` | `LIKE '%John%'` | | `dnc` | The `dnc` operator is used to exclude records where the specified text is present within the column value. This is useful for removing unwanted matching records. | `employee_name does not contain 'John'` | `NOT LIKE '%John%'` | | `sw` | The `sw` operator is used to filter records where the column value starts with the specified text entered by the user. | `employee_name starts with 'A'` | `LIKE 'A%'` | | `ew` | The `ew` operator is used to filter records where the column value ends with the specified text. | `employee_name ends with 'Kumar'` | `LIKE '%Kumar'` | | `btw` | The `btw` operator is used to filter records between two specified values. This operator is commonly used for numeric ranges, dates, and salary ranges. | `salary between 10000 and 50000` | `BETWEEN value1 AND value2` |
**How can I use Custom Filter Criteria in Grid Utils?**
Custom Filter Criteria in Grid Utils allows users to dynamically apply filtering conditions to grid data based on specific business requirements. Users can configure custom comparison operators, filter values, and logical conditions to retrieve precise records from the grid.
**Purpose of Custom Filter Criteria**
Custom filtering helps users to:
* Apply dynamic search conditions * Perform advanced grid filtering * Retrieve specific records efficiently * Combine multiple filter conditions * Customize grid search behavior
**Code Snippet:**
<#noparse> <#if (loggedInUserRoleList?seq_index_of("PRESALES") > -1)> opportunity_owner_id= "${loggedInUserId}" OR lower(created_by)= lower("${loggedInUserName}") OR user_dep_id LIKE "%${loggedInUserId}%" <#else> SELECT 0 <!--#if--><!--#noparse-->
**Step 6 :- **There is provision for viewing the grid in mobile also. Attached are the code snippets and images. ![](/cf/files/1bbca31c-b0a8-43e2-b108-601c90130ece) ![](/cf/files/916c1037-703b-4d8b-971f-d39db6ef17f1) ![](/cf/files/0dcb46c9-2000-4a2c-aa2d-a2a823d30bff) ![](/cf/files/8da9e6de-8e9e-4709-9859-2a27250450d3)

User Management is a system for authenticating users and storing user data. Our User Management APIs make managing users of any application easy. These APIs handle user registration process, user authentication, and password management. We provide a variety of login options including Database Authentication, LDAP, OAuth and SAML integration. Using our User Management module, you can manage all aspects of your user accounts such as changing user properties, reset passwords, enable or disable users, manage user profiles, and more.
<u>**Steps to enable User Management**</u>
**Step 1: **Navigate to User Management from home page.
**Step 2: **Enable the Authentication toggle button. Upon enabling, the pop-up form is opened where in the user has to enter his/her details like First Name, Last Name and Email Id. After entering the email id, and clicking on save button, a mail would be triggered to the user's mail id for logging in. ![](/cf/files/42865659-aa0b-41ec-9150-dbb5189df786)
**Step 3: **Choose the Authentication Type from the dropdown. ![](/cf/files/60d7a5b6-2338-4142-95e0-b3bcd79c7944)
There are 4 types of authentication:
1) Database 2) LDAP 3) OAuth 4) SAML
<u>**Database Authentication ** </u> For Database Authentication, the following details should be given like verification type like 1) OTP 2) Password 3) TOTP If Password is chosen as Verification Type then then by default Password Expiry is set to 30 days. By default the Regex Validation is enabled, Expression text box contains the Regex Expression for Passowrd validation and the message Text box contains the validation message which would be displayed if validation fails. There is a provision for enabling captcha validation during authentication. You can also give any desired Display Name which would be displayed in the login screen.
![](/cf/files/19058884-bf26-47ae-9e90-352c69ebe543)
<u>**OTP **</u>- Email Id + OTP login
If OTP is chosen as Verification Type:
![](/cf/files/d512723b-5234-48c6-8981-68986e3dbbd1)
![](/cf/files/d1964d46-c60d-4f92-9d27-db0d234fe243) ![](/cf/files/90b3dd04-3fcd-4172-be7e-265658f8256f)
By clicking on the send OTP button, an email would be sent to the user's email id ![](/cf/files/062399fe-986c-4ce4-b1c2-17a57c2a0953)
After that by entering the OTP received in mail, you can log into the application.
<u>** TOTP **</u>- Email Id + TOTP login
![](/cf/files/f072cbdd-5ec6-46cc-b25a-567a3bee20e3) ![](/cf/files/bdbb2a8b-308e-4dfc-8914-195511acac19)
After this you have to configure TOTP by clicking on the link Not Configured? Click here as shown in the above image. After this it gets redirected to Reset your password page where you have to enter your email id to receive the TOTP configuration. ![](/cf/files/72b2d24b-9adf-4ddc-ac3b-215ef2921c28) ![](/cf/files/2b2e833c-865e-484d-bbe8-5dc977a961cc)
**Step 4: **Then save the configuration.
![](/cf/files/8b2d5d39-8610-4e5d-b267-23248793530a)
**Step 5: **You can add multiple authentication based on your requirement by clicking on the Add button as shown in the above image and save the configuration.
<u>**OAuth**</u> : There are 3 clients for signing through OAuth . They are as follows 1) Google 2) Facebook 3) Office365
<u>**Google OAuth Steps** </u>-
1) Go to https://console.developers.google.com/apis/credentials
2) Click on project
![](/cf/files/4028b881765fd65701766028c6f60001)
3) Add project name and and keep location as default value, then click on create
![](/cf/files/4028b881765fd6570176602a9c080002)
4) Click on "+ Create Credentials" > O Auth client Id > Click on configure consent screen * Select on external user type > create button >
![](/cf/files/4028b881765fd6570176602ca2d50003)
* Add mandatory fields in above step and keep rest of the steps as default and continue
![](/cf/files/4028b881765fd6570176602d10ac0004)
* Click on Credentials tab > Click on + new credentials > O Auth client Id > select web application and add application name 1) Scroll down and add google redirect uri 'baseURL'+'contexpath'+/login/oauth2/code/google 2)Click on create after that a pop up would be displayed with client id and secret id > copy it
![](/cf/files/4028b881765fd6570176609447520005)
* These are the credentials or the data that the user can enter in UI for enabling Google Oauth Authentication.
| **Name** | **Value** | | ---------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Display Name** | Google | | **Registration Id** | google | | **Client Id** | 161594648767-g31lckb911bk3hoel8lm1aipe0lv1ttl.apps.googleusercontent.com | | **Client Secret** | GOCSPX-T1D1HrLtg5TZTsZVfea74RiEPc\_d | | **Authorization URI** | [https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth](https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth) | | **Token URI** | [https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v4/token](https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v4/token) | | **Scope** | email,profile,openid | | **Authorization Code** | CODE | | **Redirect URI** | ['baseURL'+'contexpath'/login/oauth2/code/google](http://localhost:8060/demo/login/oauth2/code/google) | | **JWK URI** | [https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v3/certs](https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v3/certs) | | **User Name Attr** | SUB | | **Token Name** | *(blank)* | | **User Info URI** | [https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v2/userinfo](https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v2/userinfo) | | **Image Path** | *(blank)* |
<u>**Facebook Oauth Steps**</u> - 1) Go to https://developers.facebook.com and register for Facebook Oauth Authenication.
* These are the credentials or the data that the user can enter in UI for enabling Facebook Oauth Authentication.
| **Name** | **Value** | | ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Display Name** | Facebook | | **Registration Id** | facebook | | **Client Id** | 610925345214462 | | **Client Secret** | 158388fec49df6c88b267767f631d7fd | | **Authorization URI** | [https://www.facebook.com/v14.0/dialog/oauth](https://www.facebook.com/v14.0/dialog/oauth) | | **Token URI** | [https://graph.facebook.com/v14.0/oauth/access\_token](https://graph.facebook.com/v14.0/oauth/access_token) | | **Scope** | public\_profile,email | | **Authorization Code** | code | | **Redirect URI** | 'baseURL'+'contextPath'/login/oauth2/code/facebook]('baseURL'+'contextPath'/login/oauth2/code/facebook) | | **JWK URI** | [https://www.facebook.com/.well-known/jwks.json](https://www.facebook.com/.well-known/jwks.json) | | **User Name Attr** | id | | **Token Name** | *(blank)* | | **User Info URI** | [https://graph.facebook.com/me?fields=id,name,email](https://graph.facebook.com/me?fields=id,name,email) | | **Image Path** | *(blank)* |
<u>**Office365 Oauth Steps**</u> - * Click on https://portal.azure.com/ and complete the registration for Office365. * After completeing the registration, enter the credentials in the UI.
* These are the credentials or the data that the user can enter in UI for enabling Office365 Oauth Authentication.
| Name | Value | |------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Display Name** | office365 | | **Registration Id** | office365 | | ** Client Id** | 8974f818-1d93-441a-84cf-94ec6bad88d6 | | **Client Secret** | i0m8Q~1gLXEPapxXzmythn6L4vM1FJzV2yxDea1j | | **Authorization URI** | https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/authorize | | **Token URI** | https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/token | | **Authorization Code** | code | | **Redirect URI** | 'baseURL'+'contextPath'/login/oauth2/code/office365 | | **JWK URI** | https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/discovery/keys | | **User Name Attr** | SUB | | **Token Name** | (blank) | | **User Info URI** | (blank) | | **Image Path** | (blank) | | **Office365 Log Out URL** | https://login.microsoftonline.com/office365/oauth2/v2.0/logout | | **Office365 Base URL** | 'baseURL'+'contextPath' |
<u>**SAML:**</u>
<u>**Steps for enabling SAML Authentication:**</u>
** Step 1: ** Use HTTPS instead of HTTP in the URL.
** Step 2: ** Following are the steps to configure HTTPS in your springboot application: 1. Create a Self-Signed Certificate. keytool -genkey -alias tomcat -storetype PKCS12 -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -keystore keystore.p12 -validity 3650 The above command should be in single line or else some error will be thrown.
![](/cf/files/2b00768c-6d3b-4e21-9c38-e6764de990a4)
This code will generate a PKCS12 keystore file named as keystore.p12 and the certificate alias name is springBoot.
2. Configure HTTPS We need to provide key-store file path, key-store-password, key-store-type and key alias name into the application.properties/yml file. Any port number can be used.
![](/cf/files/d5d4098a-3ac8-4bfd-a53a-bddeca3be413)
Note: Store the keystore.p12 in the jwsmaven-common-utilsjava-web-startersrcmain resources folder i.e referred to as classpath.
Then restart the application, use the URL: 'baseURL'+'contextPath' You can choose your convenient context-path.
4. Identity Provider Configuration (Okta) The Identity Provider used is Okta, so after creating a free Developer account, let’s create our Application. Follow the steps:
![](/cf/files/68812ef5-9384-4f6c-be0f-b814d12cd5fb)
Click Add Applications in the top right of the Dashboard to continue. This will bring you to a screen with a Create New App green button on the left of the screen.
![](/cf/files/bbee2a7a-99ef-42c9-9f23-53bad117fab8)
Choose SAML 2.0 for the sign on method. The next screen will take you to the General configuration page.
![](/cf/files/d2d8ee5b-ccf6-4540-aceb-257461346cba)
For configuring the SAML screen. Enter the following values:
![](/cf/files/2ddbb6f7-a479-4aa3-9035-16d101567631)
Then Choose “I’m an Okta customer adding an internal app” and optionally select the checkbox of the App type.
![](/cf/files/60afcd54-19b2-44d8-880b-c2407b168c40)
Copy the Identity Provider metadata link from the yellow box as you’ll need it during the configuration of your Spring Boot application.
![](/cf/files/7c5f5b84-d4bb-4e6a-99e0-571701486dc8)
Click on the Assignments screen/tab and the Assign > Assign to People button to add the users.
![](/cf/files/467f02a8-e2b5-49a6-a441-b99280887972)
After all this you need to set the First Name, Last Name and Email in the Attributes section. These information is needed in the SAML Assertion Response for Authentication. This is available in the second tab i.e Configure SAML.
![](/cf/files/795650d6-1c12-4b45-a03d-75f897198e31) ![](/cf/files/b05fa373-b29a-43a1-ae2f-3601ba5966d7)
So, after following the above mentioned steps, we are set to use Okta as IdP in our application. After this, enable Authentication by going to User Management Module and enable SAML authentication. After saving and restarting , you need to provide the following credentials in the UI Screen. For Logging Out of Okta and redirecting to home page, you need to provide two inputs. 1. SAML Log Out URL : https://app-domain.okta.com/login/signout E.g.: https://dev-21383837.okta.com/login/signout 2. SAML Base URL: Your application base URL E.g.:'baseURL'+'contextPath'
<u>**Here the credentials are as follows that needs to be entered in UI:**</u>
| **Name** | **Value** | | ----------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Display Name** | SAML | | **Registration Id** | okta | | **Assertion Consumer Service Location** | ['baseURL'+'contextPath'/login/saml2/sso/okta]('baseURL'+'contextPath'/login/saml2/sso/okta) | | **Meta Data URL** | [https://dev-21388387.okta.com/app/exkmhwesbmJOSQ7h45d7/sso/saml/metadata](https://dev-21388387.okta.com/app/exkmhwesbmJOSQ7h45d7/sso/saml/metadata) | | **IdP Web SSO URL** | [https://dev-21388387.okta.com/app/dev-21388387\_jquiver\_1/exkmhwesbmJOSQ7h45d7/sso/saml](https://dev-21388387.okta.com/app/dev-21388387_jquiver_1/exkmhwesbmJOSQ7h45d7/sso/saml) | | **SAML Log Out URL** | [https://dev-21388387.okta.com/login/signout](https://dev-21388387.okta.com/login/signout) | | **SAML Base URL** | ['baseURL'+'contextPath']('baseURL'+'contextPath') |
<u>**LDAP** </u>
<u>**Steps for enabling LDAP Authentication**</u>
** Step 1: ** You need to have access to VPN.
** Step 2: ** You need to provide the following configurations.
| **Name** | **Value** | | -------------------- | ------------------------ | | **Display Name** | LDAP | | **Server Host** | 192.168.102.6 | | **Server Port** | 389 | | **Security Level** | Simple | | **Base Domain Name** | DC=trigyn,DC=com | | **Admin User Name** | User Mail Id | | **Password** | ******** | | **Login Attribute** | mail | | **Search Scope** | Subtree | | **Search Filter** | OU=Trigyn\_Mumbai\_users |
**After enabling LDAP Authentication, the login screen will look like the picture below:**
![](/cf/files/f6a6d95f-c5c4-4bf2-b21e-7e7edac001ce)
<u>**User Management module provides the following features: **</u> * Manage roles * Manage users * Manage permissions
![](/cf/files/ed8eeba0-7381-4d01-bd8d-6d13165e6f02)
<u>** Manage roles: **</u> By clicking on Manage Roles icon, you can navigate to the Role Master Listing page. ![](/cf/files/3323e9a9-075e-41f5-a1b6-0bce405da169)
There are 3 predefined system roles : ADMIN, AUTHENTICATED, ANONYMOUS which you cant edit.
**Step 1: ** By clicking on the Add Role button, you can add new Role in the Add Role Page and save it. ![](/cf/files/40232f8b-c302-4d82-b155-c92b21600022)
You can add/edit role and set as active or inactive depending on your use case.
<u>** Manage users : **</u> By clicking on Manage users icon, you can navigate to the User Master Listing page. There is 1 predefined user which cant be modified : admin user While add/edit user by default AUTHENTICATED role will be assigned and disabled.
** Step 1: **By clicking on the Add User button, you can add new User in the Add User Page and save it. ![](/cf/files/501b9200-a556-436c-8474-33c14b3c1593) ![](/cf/files/f2c32ac3-eb0f-46c9-9007-392da106c74f)
You need to enter basic user fields "name", "email" etc.. and assign role. You can active/deactivate a user through "is active" field. You can configure whether to send mail or not while a user is created/updated, using Send Mail option. If you enable "force user to change password" field then a mail would be sent to the user mail id for changing the password, mandatorily. You wont be able to off the Send Mail property, in this case. The template used for email is : "force-password-mail-subject" and "force-password-mail" You can modify if you need to alter it.
<u>**Manage Permissions: **</u> By clicking on Manage Permissions icon, you can navigate to the Manage Permissions page. ![](/cf/files/a56d471a-2aa0-4355-a166-aff429db4d3c)
It contains all the entites which are made up from various modules present in the application if we want to remove a particular entity access just uncheck it from the grid for that role. Only those modules will be visible whose module_type_id = 0 is in the table jq_entity_role_association
Use this section to quickly navigate to practical guides for performing specific tasks like embedding templates, creating forms, validating data, and configuring system features.
By using Grid Utils, you can enhance data visibility, improve user interaction, and streamline operations on structured data.

The Logical Operators feature in Grid Utils allows users to build advanced filtering conditions while searching or filtering grid data. These operators help combine multiple conditions to retrieve more precise results.
manualEntries.entryName: Use Logical Operators in Grid Utils?

manualEntries.entryContent:
Use the routing configuration to define a new route that connects a URL path to a specific module, page, or functionality within the application. Navigate to the Router section and create a new route by specifying the path, target component, and any required parameters.
You can also configure access controls, navigation behavior, and dynamic parameters to handle different use cases. Once added, the route enables users to navigate directly to the defined page using the specified URL.
Adding routes helps organize application navigation, improve user flow, and ensure seamless access to different features.

A dashboard is a page that consists of multiple components, mostly independent of each other. Dashboards are reusable, customizable, can display different types of information at one place. It is an information management tool that receives data from a linked database to provide data visualizations. Dashboard use data visualization to help businesses make real-time decisions. They help to create the daily reporting, application usage, trends dashboard for web application and controls it with the dashboard admin panel. Dashboards can help ensure that everyone has access to the latest data and insights, which can lead to better decision-making. Dashboards can be customized, which allows different people within an organization to use the same data in different ways. Dashboards can display information in real-time, which can help decision makers act quickly.
<u>**Steps for creating a Dashboard are as follows:**</u>
**Step 1: **Navigate to Dashboard from the home page.
After step 1 Dashlets needs to be created to be included in the Dashboard. # Dashlet A dashlet is an individual component that can be added to or removed from a dashboard. They are a reusable unit of functionality. When the dashboard loads it should just show the UI of the dashlets . After the page load each dashlet sends a server call (based on its type) to fetch its data. After the data has been fetched, each dashlet (based on its type) renders the data. Dashlets render the information in UI that needs to be displayed through the dashboards . Information can be of various types like Server Listing, CPU Usage etc. as per the user’s requirement. Dashboards are containers consisting of multiple dashlets. They control the way the dashlets are organized in dashboards.
<u>**Steps for creating a Dashlet are as follows:**</u>
**Step 1: **Click on Manage Dashlets after navigating to Dashboard page as shown in the picture below.
![](/cf/files/fd119b35-a6fe-476d-9561-46b2a7c90341)
**Step 2: **Click on Create New Dashlet
![](/cf/files/51931155-0c10-43e9-bd3d-4bde6aee0431)
**Step 3: **Create New Dashlet by providing all necessary information
Give the Dashlet Name, Title, Co-ordinates, Dimensions etc. ![](/cf/files/b19e9cbb-b05a-4e29-a398-0feff561a0e4)
**Note:** Details about Configurable properties is listed below in the manual.
In the HTML Script Editor, the content that is to be displayed on the dashlet should be written. In this picture given below,here the count of the total number of grids that are present in the table, is displayed. The count is obtained from the query that is executed in the Select Script editor in a resultSet object. ![](/cf/files/65fc7e48-cda1-4661-8437-dea3533c4a54)
This is the select script editor, where the queries are written whose result is displayed on the dashlet. Here, the queryType can be Select Script or Javascript(default value).Based on the queryType the content in the Select Script editor is displayed. ![](/cf/files/8e530a09-c3b1-4197-a49e-3d220f4e063e)
After entering the values, save the dashlet.
**Step 4: **Navigate back to Dashboard listing page and click on copy button to copy the dashlet Id. ![](/cf/files/3b7ea334-d513-4827-bd5a-4ff88f08d571)
**Step 5: **You can edit the dashlet in the edit mode. In order to make the dashlet active, you need to enable the Dashlet Active toggle button and for including header, you need to enable the Dashlet Header toggle button as shown in the picture below.
![](/cf/files/cbeb00be-4b05-4d77-89b6-8242b85eb3ac)
**Step 6: **User can also add provide additional configurable properties to a dashlet like no of records to be visible at a time or you can allow end user to enter a name or age which will be taken into consideration while fetching records. User also need to provide HTML content and SQL query.
![](/cf/files/0898958c-8cce-4ac3-8f6f-1ce5b886acfe)
**Step 7: **Navigate back to Dashboard listing page and click on Create New Dashboard button for creating a dashboard.
![](/cf/files/45ab6497-5953-4120-b05b-ea9365ebe648)
**Step 8: **Create new dashboard by providing all neccessary information and also include the dashlet created before. User has an option to make a dashboard draggable by enabling the Draggable Dashboard toggle button. User can also include Dashboard header based on his/her requirement.
![](/cf/files/60905a88-b892-4b9b-9e9c-0b4682ebef9a)
**Step 9: **Dashboard created will be displayed in the listing page.
![](/cf/files/f6088a79-2a9a-428f-bf41-955bf8b72f19)
**Step 10: ** By clicking on the eye icon of the dashboard in the listing page , user can view the dashlet included in the dashboard as shown below.
![](/cf/files/588f22d8-ea20-4345-b825-e564444a8322)
**Step 11: **By clicking on the arrow button of the displayed dashlet user can refresh the dashlet in every desired secs/minutes depending on the requirement. This will automatically refresh the dashlets based on the time set.
![](/cf/files/8fce9b14-8a4e-491e-b8e0-100462515b40) ![](/cf/files/75db071a-43e2-4578-b0a4-38c919a3bca6) ![](/cf/files/8e66f0dd-5d85-4b93-b8a6-e045f19435a7)
**Step 12: **By clicking on the filter button of the displayed dashlet user can modify the configurable properties and save the changes as shown in the picture.
![](/cf/files/197e8754-a4d2-4265-a322-cbed789324c0) ![](/cf/files/f71f5cfb-0457-4b62-9177-5d223ff95aaa)
**Step 13: ** For displaying the configurable properties in the dashlet the following changes have to be done in the HTML editor of the dashlet. ![](/cf/files/58c9e26e-c4ca-4eb4-90e2-a4d740545c5a)
**Step 14: ** After making the changes, the changes will be reflected in the displayed dashlet.
![](/cf/files/7d339fde-6e6f-47c3-bd25-46b98eaa96bb)
**Step 15: ** There is a provision also for the user to change the value of the configurable properties from the console by calling refreshDashletContent method and passing the values to be changed as shown in the picture. For this user needs to provide the dashlet id which he/she can get by clicking on the copy button of the required dashlet in the listing page.
![](/cf/files/32494c32-ddcf-4d77-b621-c1fdcc7449ad) ![](/cf/files/3a946afd-e5c8-477a-bbf8-89f7e4ea6099)

Content Security Policy (CSP) issues occur when the browser blocks scripts, styles, images, or other resources that violate the application's security policy. Proper CSP configuration helps secure the application while allowing trusted resources to load correctly.

Custom Filter Criteria in Grid Utils allows users to dynamically apply filtering conditions to grid data based on specific business requirements. Users can configure custom comparison operators, filter values, and logical conditions to retrieve precise records from the grid.
manualEntries.entryName: Use Custom Filter Criteria in Grid Utils?

manualEntries.entryContent:
Use Form.io to design and manage dynamic forms through a visual interface without extensive coding. You can drag and drop components such as text fields, dropdowns, checkboxes, and layouts to quickly create forms tailored to your requirements.
Form.io allows you to configure validations, default values, conditional logic, and data bindings for each component. Once designed, forms can be embedded into your application and connected to APIs for data submission and processing.
This approach simplifies form creation, enables rapid customization, and ensures consistent handling of user input across the application.

Caching improves application performance by storing frequently accessed data temporarily, reducing repeated database queries and expensive operations. In Spring Boot applications, caching can be configured directly through the application.yml file.

Custom exception handling allows applications to display meaningful and user-friendly error messages instead of generic system errors. It helps improve debugging, error tracking, and overall user experience by handling exceptions in a controlled manner.
Internationalization helps make the application accessible to a wider audience by providing a localized and user-friendly experience across different regions.

This utility layer provides JavaScript-accessible functions to interact with various system components such as cookies, sessions, files, database, and more via com.trigyn.jws.dynarest.cipher.utils.ScriptUtil.
🧩 <u>**General Setup**</u> * Java Class Used: com.trigyn.jws.dynarest.cipher.utils.ScriptUtil
* JavaScript Interface: All methods are accessible via the jq_ prefix.
**<u>Instantiation:</u>**
```var jq_scriptUtilClass = Java.type('com.trigyn.jws.dynarest.cipher.utils.ScriptUtil'); var jq_scriptUtilObject = new jq_scriptUtilClass();```
🔧 <u>**System Property & Environment**</u>
| Function | Description | | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------- | | `jq_getSystemProperty(key)` | Returns a JVM system property. | | `jq_getSystemEnvironment(key)` | Returns an OS-level environment variable. |
🍪<u>** Cookie Management**</u>
| Function | Description | | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------ | | `jq_updateCookies(key, value)` | Adds or updates a cookie. | | `jq_updateCookies(key, value, maxAge)` | Adds/updates a cookie with expiration. | | `jq_updateCookies(key, value, httpOnly)` | Adds/updates a cookie with HTTP-only flag. | | `jq_updateCookies(key, value, maxAge, httpOnly)` | Full customization of cookie properties. | | `jq_updateCookieSecurity(key, isSecured)` | Toggles `Secure` flag on a cookie. | | `jq_getCookiesFromRequest(key)` | Fetches a cookie by key. | | `jq_haveCookie(key)` | Checks if a cookie exists. | | `jq_deleteCookie(key)` | Deletes a specific cookie. |
🧵 <u>**Session Management**</u>
| Function | Description | | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------- | | `jq_updateSession(key, value)` | Adds/updates session attribute. | | `jq_getValueFromSession(key)` | Retrieves session attribute. | | `jq_haveSessionKey(key)` | Checks if session key exists. | | `jq_deleteSessionKey(key)` | Removes session attribute. | | `jq_getCreationTime()` | Returns session creation timestamp. | | `jq_getLastAccessedTime()` | Returns last accessed timestamp. |
📁 <u>**File Operations**</u>
| Function | Description | | ------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | | `jq_getAllFiles(filePath)` | Lists files from given directory path. | | `jq_deleteFile(filePath)` | Deletes a file from disk. | | `jq_saveFile(fileContent, targetFileName)` | Saves content to a file. | | `jq_saveFileFromPath(filePath, fileBinID, contextID)` | Saves a file from path to DB/FileBin. | | `jq_saveFileBin(fileContent, fileName, fileBinID, contextID)` | Saves content into file bin. | | `jq_getFileContent(path)` | Reads file content as string. | | `jq_getFileBinContent(fileUploadID)` | Fetches content from file bin using ID. | | `jq_copyFile(src, dest)` | Copies a file. | | `jq_copyFile(src, dest, targetFileName)` | Copies and renames a file. | | `jq_copyFileBinId(fileUploadID, destPath)` | Copies file bin to a file path. |
🛢️ <u>**Database Operations**</u>
| Function | Description | | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------ | | `jq_getDBResult(query, dataSourceID, requestParams)` | Executes SELECT query. | | `jq_callStoredProcedure(query, dataSourceID, requestParams)` | Executes a stored procedure. | | `jq_updateDBQuery(query, dataSourceID, requestParams)` | Executes INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE. |
🌐 <u>**REST, Mail, Template**</u>
| Function | Description | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | | `jq_executeRESTCall(restXML)` | Makes REST API call from XML config. | | `jq_sendMail(mailXML)` | Sends email based on XML template. | | `jq_evalTemplateByName(templateName, requestParams)` | Evaluates FreeMarker template by name. | | `jq_evalTemplateByContent(templateContent, requestParams)` | Evaluates FreeMarker template content. | | `jq_convertToPDFFromTemplate(templateName, contextMap, imageFolder)` | Generates PDF from template. | | `jq_convertToPDFFromString(sourceHTML, imageFolder)` | Generates PDF from raw HTML. |
📝 <u>**Logging and Notifications**</u>
| Function | Description | | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------- | | `jq_logActivity(requestParams)` | Logs an action or event. | | `jq_addNotifications(requestParams)` | Sends notification entry. |
⚠️ <u>**Error Handling**</u>
| Function | Description | | ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | | `jq_sendError(statusCode, message)` | Sends a response with error code/message. |
📎<u>** URL Path Variables**</u>
| Function | Description | | ------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- | | `jq_getPathVariableSet()` | Returns all path variables. | | `jq_getPathVariable()` | Returns single path variable (if only one exists). |
**<u>Details about jq_sendError and jq_getPathVariable and jq_getPathVariableSet methods given.</u>**
<u>** jq_sendError : **</u> The jq_sendError function provides a unified way to raise custom errors from the client-side or scripting logic to the backend, halting further execution and passing meaningful error codes and messages.
<u>**Function Signature (Client-Side Script): **</u>
``` jq_sendError(statusCode, errorMessage)```
* **statusCode:** HTTP-like status code (e.g., 400, 403, 500) indicating the type of error.
* **errorMessage:** Custom message to describe the cause of the error (e.g., "Error while saving the query"). Applies default error code (400) and message (jws.defaultErrorMessage) if values are null or empty.
**<u>Example Usage:</u>**
``` jq_sendError("403", "Error while saving the query.");```
This method is called during:
* Invalid user input.
* Failed database operations.
* Missing required data.
* Validates inputs.
So, this method throws a CustomStopException with a statusCode i.e 403 and an error message like Error while saving the query.
**<u>Key Features:</u>** **<u>Centralized Error Handling:</u>** * Ensures all errors follow a consistent format and are handled uniformly across modules.
**<u>Stops Further Processing:</u>** * By throwing CustomStopException, it halts further script or process execution where failure occurs.
**<u>Supports Custom Messages:</u>** * Allows developers to supply specific, user-friendly error messages to help with debugging or UI feedback.
**<u>Default Fallbacks:</u>** * Applies a default error code/message when missing, ensuring no silent failures.
**<u>Can be Intercepted in Frontend/UI:</u>** * These exceptions can be caught and displayed on UI as notifications, alerts, or modal messages.
<u>**jq_getPathVariable and jq_getPathVariableSet:**</u>
These utility functions are used to retrieve path variables from the current HTTP request URL in a dynamic REST API module. These variables are parts of the URL not covered by the static pattern defined in the database.
They are especially useful when working with dynamic API paths like:
/test/a/123/abc
Where the static pattern stored in DB might be:
test/a/**
<u>**jq_getPathVariable():**</u> Returns the dynamic segment(s) of the request URI that match the * or **.
This method is included in the Monaco Editor's suggestion.
By calling this method jq_getPathVariable() , the dynamic path variables from the url can be retrieved.
**<u>Example Usage:</u>**
```var pathVariable = jq_getPathVariable();```
And the url is http://localhost:8060/demo/api/test/a/123/abc
and the Rest API Url is : test/a/**
Then it will print pathVariable as 123/abc
<u>**jq_getPathVariableSet():**</u> Returns the dynamic segment(s) of the request URI that match the * or **.
This method is included in the Monaco Editor's suggestion.
By calling this method jq_getPathVariable() , the dynamic path variables from the url can be retrieved.
**<u>Example Usage:</u>**
```var pathVariable = jq_getPathVariableSet();```
And the url is http://localhost:8060/demo/api/test/a/123/abc
and the Rest API Url is : test/a/**
Then it will print pathVariable as [123, abc]
This Link will take you to the content of How To Embed a Template.

Enable User Management to control access, roles, and permissions for users within the application. Navigate to the User Management module and configure user accounts by defining roles, assigning permissions, and managing user details.

Objective of this project to rapdily develop Enterprise Web Application. Ideally in normal situation anyone with basic knowledge of HTML, JavaScript, JQuery, CSS & SQL (MariaDB / MySQL / PostgreSQL) will be able to work on the project. So people from different development segment will be able to work together. Also the development is live development. Developers can work right on the SIT system. While the manager can monitor the development live. Trigyn has been working with number of Web Application project, which consists of components like Grids, Forms, Templating. In-order to speed up the initial project setup and components configuration there was a need to develop a reusable component.
# Modules Maven Group-id :- com.trigyn 1. **Master Generator** : -Master Generator plays a pivotal role while creating an application by using JQuiver framework. Helps to create any master modules without any much effort. It helps to design the Listing page, Form Builder and Router from the table available in the corresponding DB, to support the application. 2. **Grid Utils** : - (gridutils) While developing any web application, there is always a need to show listing page, wherein the user can sort the data, filter it and keeping the performance of application in mind the data should be loaded lazily. 3. **TypeAhead/Autocomplete** : - Built using frontend library, rich autocomplete to get data lazily or dependent dropdown, easy custom UI.Developer just need to write SQL query and frontend component will start working. 4. **File Bins** : - Create File Bins for the application for uploading files. Users can include file bin in any form, template or module and upload multiple files of any format, size as per their requirements. There is a provision for Custom File Storage also. 5. **Templating** : - (templating) Many web applications contain certain structure or pattern to be followed while display views, sending out mails etc. A template is a form or pattern used as a guide to make something. They can be of various types like form templates, listing templates, email templates and rest-client templates. Generates HTML web pages, e-mails, configuration files, etc. from template files and the data your application provides. 6. **Form Builder** : - (dynamicform) Suppose there is a need to build a simple form, where you have to perform CRUD operations on one or two entities (usually for master tables), you can use this module provided by JQuiver.The workstation where you can create, edit, and manage a form can be called as a form builder. Forms collect different types of data through the fields present in them. These fields can be managed and customized from its form builder. Helps to create dynamic forms for the web application, without writing any java code just by using free marker, java script, python, php, RoR, Java etc. At one place developer can write the display and persistence business logic. 7. **REST API** : - (dynamic-rest) Representational State Transfer (REST) is an architectural style that defines a set of constraints to be used for creating web services. REST API is a way of accessing web services in a simple and flexible way without having any processing. REST APIs are efficient, high-performing, consume less bandwidth, and are cost-effective because developers can use them without third-party tools. One REST API can be published in 5 minutes. 8. **Router** : - Routing is the module that maps the UI tree nodes to the URL paths and vice versa. Each branch of the tree is assigned a path. The route (URL path) to a node is obtained by joining all paths from root to the node by /. This assignment drives UI navigation in the sense that the UI view for a given URL path consists of the UI view snapshot mentioned above by composing all web components on the path. i.e., for that URL path, only the web components on the path are in the DOM and contribute to the UI rendering. It helps in creating menu for any application effectively, efficiently and effortlessly. This also have a DnD UI to adjust menu. 9. **Internationalization** : - (resourcebundle) The process of designing web applications in such a way that which provides support for various countries, various languages, and various currencies automatically without performing any change in the application is called Internationalization(I18N). It is known as I18N because between I and N; there are 18 characters; that’s why I18N. It helps to display messages, currencies, dates, time, etc., according to the specific region or language. Features for Importing and Exporting Resource Keys is given in the module. The keys are exported and imported in files in the excel format. 10. **Dashboard** : -A dashboard is a page that consists of multiple components, mostly independent of each other. Dashboards are reusable, customizable, can display different types of information at one place. It is an information management tool that receives data from a linked database to provide data visualizations. Dashboard use data visualization to help businesses make real-time decisions. They help to create the daily reporting, application usage, trends dashboard for web application and controls it with the dashboard admin panel. Dashboards can help ensure that everyone has access to the latest data and insights, which can lead to better decision-making. Dashboards can be customized, which allows different people within an organization to use the same data in different ways. Dashboards can display information in real-time, which can help decision makers act quickly. 11. **Dashlet** : -A dashlet is an individual component that can be added to or removed from a dashboard. They are a reusable unit of functionality. When the dashboard loads it should just show the UI of the dashlets . x After the page load each dashlet sends a server call (based on its type) to fetch its data. After the data has been fetched, each dashlet (based on its type) renders the data. Dashlets render the information in UI that needs to be displayed through the dashboards . Information can be of various types like Server Listing, CPU Usage etc. as per the user’s requirement. Dashboards are containers consisting of multiple dashlets. They control the way the dashlets are organized in dashboards. 12. **Notification** : - (notification) A notification module is a software component that manages, creates, and sends notifications to users. When an event occurs, the module generates a message based on predefined rules or templates. It helps to create application notification with ease and control the duration and context where to show it, (cross platform). 13. **User Management** : - User Management is a system for authenticating users and storing user data. Our User Management APIs make managing users of any application easy. These APIs handle user registration process, user authentication, and password management. We provide a variety of login options including Database Authentication, LDAP, OAuth and SAML integration. Using our User Management module, you can manage all aspects of your user accounts such as changing user properties, reset passwords, enable or disable users, manage user profiles, and more. 14. **Application Configuration** : -JQuiver provides with a database level implementation of application configurations, wherein user can manage all environment and system related properties.JQuiver has in-built classes and methods to configure mails. To enable mail configuration , "Mail Configuration" page is provided in the Application Configuration Module. Here, the details like, SMTP Host, SMTP Port, Security Protocol, as per the user mail server is provided. 15. **Help Manual** : - A help manual, also known as a user manual or instruction manual, is a document that provides instructions and information for using a product. It can help users learn how to install, troubleshoot, and operate a product.It provides detailed information about a module which can be helpful for users to troubleshoot their issues on their own, which reduces the workload on customer support and also save time. 16. **Form IO** : - Form.ios Developer Portal provides you with the drag-and-drop Form Builder that automatically generates the necessary API. 17. **Import and Export** : - This module provides provision for importing and exporting different modules and configurations based on the user’s requirement.The file is exported and imported in zip format. The permissions along with the modules are also imported. Many user friendly options are given. 18. **Script Library** :- A script library allows you to store classes, functions, and variables for common use within an application. A script library is either a Client Library or a Server Library. The scope of a script library is the current application.It can be included in the REST API Module, Form Builder Module and File Bins Module. It is optional based on the user’s requirement it should be included. Unlike source files for other programming languages that must be compiled into bytecode before you run them, scripts are evaluated by a runtime environment (in this case, by a script engine) directly. Provisions for JavaScript, Python and PHP Script Engines are provided in the application. 19. **Custom File Storage** : - A custom file storage module is a programmatic system or library built to handle the creation, storage, retrieval, modification, and deletion of files. Instead of relying on a pre-built library or system, this module is designed with custom logic tailored to meet specific requirements of an application or project. A custom file storage module is an excellent choice for applications with unique requirements, offering control and efficiency that pre-built solutions might not provide. The module typically abstracts file operations, allowing developers to interact with files in a structured and secure manner. You can tailor the module to fit specific business needs, such as custom directory structures, unique naming conventions, or specific data formats. Allows seamless integration with other components in your system. You can implement custom encryption, user authentication, and access controls, ensuring secure file handling. Reduces reliance on third-party libraries that may have security vulnerabilities. 20. **API Client** : - An API client is a set of tools and protocols that operate from an application on a computer. They help you to bypass some operations when developing a web application rather than reinventing the wheel every time. Using a client API is a great way to speed up the development process. The API Client authenticates the client making a request. It also provides authorization letting the API know if the requesting application is allowed to use the API and what it can access. 21. **Scheduler** : -Scheduler is used to schedule a thread or task to execute at a certain period of time or periodically at a fixed interval. There are multiple ways of scheduling a task in Java. Timer Task is executed by one execution demon thread. Any delay in a task can delay the other task in schedule. Our Application also has other scheduling frameworks like Quartz Scheduler which offer more advanced features and capabilities for scheduling jobs in a distributed environment. These frameworks support clustering, load balancing, and failover, making them suitable for enterprise applications. Scheduler API allows users to schedule tasks for future execution, or to run at specific intervals or at fixed times. Scheduler can be used to run background tasks, process user requests, or perform other system-related functions. 21. **Visual DnD Organiser** : -JQuiver supports drag and drop (DnD) for transferring information between components in an application. This is incorporated for handling the different Router Menus in the application.User can directly manipulate the menus created from the UI with the features of DnD Organiser. The sequence which is maintained on the UI will be maintained in the DB. 22. ** View REST API** : -This module helps to view all the custom REST APIs created in the application. Users gets an easy view of the different Data Parameters and the Response Header.Export Option is also given for exporting the selected REST APIs in PDF format. Sorting Option is also provided for the user to export in their desired sorting order.
Use the Between operator to display records whose values fall within a specified range, such as numbers, dates, or amounts.
manualEntries.entryName: Filter Records Between Two Values Using Between Operator?

manualEntries.entryContent: Docker is a platform that lets you package an application along with all its dependencies (libraries, configs, runtime) into a single unit called a container. This container can run reliably on any system that has Docker installed—your laptop, a server, or the cloud—without worrying about environment differences.

Java Melody is a monitoring tool used to track application performance, server statistics, SQL queries, memory usage, HTTP requests, and application health in Java-based applications. Enabling Java Melody helps administrators monitor and diagnose performance issues effectively.

Enable Mail Configuration to allow the system to send emails for notifications, alerts, and user communications. Configure the required mail server details such as SMTP host, port, username, and password in the Mail Settings section.

The Notification module enables administrators to configure system notifications that are displayed to users based on predefined conditions. Notifications can be scheduled for specific time periods, categorized by message type, and configured to appear either once or multiple times depending on the defined settings.
<u>**Follow the steps below to configure a new Notification.**</u>
**Step 1: **Access the Notification module from the JQuiver Home.
**Step 2: **Click Create New to create a new notification configuration.
![](/cf/files/6d450298-fc7f-43ca-a4e5-ed15f308ef2d)
**Step 3: **Provide the notification configuration details.
* Specify the Message Valid From date to define when the notification becomes active. * Specify the Message Valid Till date to define when the notification expires. * Select the Message Type to categorize the notification. Available options include Informative, Warning and Error. * Enter the Message Text that will be displayed to the user. * Enable the Display Once option if the notification should be shown only once per user session.
![](/cf/files/9be8f913-8028-435c-9add-677764237f52)
**Step 4: **Define the notification selection criteria.
