* Enter a SQL query in the Selection Criteria field to determine when the notification should be triggered. * The query must return a numerical column named noti_count. * If the returned value is greater than 0, the notification will be displayed to the user. * Dynamic queries can also be constructed using available system parameters.
* Choose the Datasource from which the notification query will be executed.
![](/cf/files/a80f39c7-99f3-448a-9246-5d6bf64f52e1)
**Step 5: **Click Save & Return to persist the notification configuration and return to the notification listing page.
![](/cf/files/bf4bc1cf-8943-4be4-b451-3367197af84a)
**Step 6: **From the Notification Listing page, the following actions are available:
* Edit – Modify the existing notification configuration.. * Delete – Remove the notification from the system.
![](/cf/files/c0e5dc11-6dd8-49c0-8145-fa7031c9a31c)
**Step 7: **Viewing Notifications
* After a notification is configured and the defined conditions are satisfied, the notification becomes available to users through the Notifications option in the application header. * In the top-right corner of the interface, the system displays a notification indicator with a count of available notifications, as shown in the image below. * When the user selects this notification indicator, a notification panel opens displaying the list of active notifications along with their corresponding message text and validity duration. * Each notification is presented with its message content and the number of remaining valid days, allowing users to review all active notifications configured in the system.
![](/cf/files/dbe38080-3528-4b2d-a07d-fc68f3bf5bda)

The Developer’s View Model module enables administrators to create and manage custom business modules by grouping and linking application entities. This module provides a centralized interface to organize related modules, define reusable business views, and simplify navigation for developers within the application.
<u>**Follow the steps below to configure a new Developer’s View Model.**</u>
**Step 1: **Access the Developer’s View Model module from the corresponding JQuiver configuration modules such as Templating, REST API, Form Builder, Form IO or other supported development modules available within the platform.
**Step 2: **Click Create/Link Module to create a new business module or link an existing module.
![](/cf/files/090e93d2-9f07-4f69-9f2c-98191d9b9afa)
**Step 3: **Create a new business module.
* Select the Create option from the Add Module popup. * Enter the Module Name that should be created. * Click Ok to create the business module temporarily in the current configuration. * Click Save & Return from the main page to persist the business module details permanently in the system.
![](/cf/files/ade0fba1-a470-4edc-bb48-39764ecb32d9)
**Step 4:** Link existing modules to the business module.
* Select the Link option from the Add Module popup. * Search and select the required module from the available module list. * Click Ok to associate the selected entity with the business module temporarily in the current configuration. * Click Save & Return from the main page to persist the linked module configuration permanently in the system.
![](/cf/files/3fc1a651-ac02-437a-ad8e-ac0a961ea43b)
**Step 5: **Access the Developer’s View Model module from the JQuiver Home.
**Step 6: **View, search and filter configured business modules.
* The left panel displays all available business modules. * Use the Search field in the left panel to quickly locate business modules. * Selecting a business module displays the linked modules and entities in the right-side listing section. * The listing contains Module Name, Entity Name and available Actions. * Click Back to return to the previous screen or navigation menu.
![](/cf/files/0bbbabff-eb7f-4af8-a789-6708b9f1ffc4)
**Step 7: **Manage linked modules using the available actions.
The following actions are available for each linked module:
* View – Opens the linked module configuration. * Remove Link – Removes the association between the entity and the business module. * History – Displays the code history details of the linked entity for tracking previous changes
![](/cf/files/0bbbabff-eb7f-4af8-a789-6708b9f1ffc4)

CK Editor is a rich text editor integrated into the application to create, edit, and format content with an easy-to-use WYSIWYG (What You See Is What You Get) interface. It allows users to design professional content without writing HTML manually.

The Reset Autocomplete functionality allows users to clear the selected or entered value from an autocomplete field and restore it to its default empty state. This helps users perform new searches or remove previously selected suggestions easily.

The Favourites feature allows users to quickly access frequently used pages, records, menus, or items by marking them as favourites. Users can add items to favourites for faster navigation and remove them when no longer needed.
Use the Equal To operator to display records that exactly match a specified value.
manualEntries.entryName: Filter Grid Data Using Equal To (=) Operator?

manualEntries.entryContent: The contain operator is used in Grid Utils to filter records that contain specific text or character values within a column. It performs partial text matching instead of exact matching, making searches more flexible and user-friendly.
manualEntries.entryName: Use contain Operator in Grid Filtering?

manualEntries.entryContent:
<u>**Follow the steps below to create a Autocomplete dynamically.**</u>
**Step 1: **Visit the TypeAhead/Autocomplete module on Jquiver home.
**Step 2 : **Click on Add Autocomplete, it will open up to add/edit Autocomplete. ![](/cf/files/7643a091-d2c5-498e-8c20-33dc2952da0b)
**Step 3 : **Fill in the Autocomplete Id and Description and choose the table for which the Autocomplete Query would be generated. ![](/cf/files/76c6d5b3-b55d-46cb-9ecf-6b101317fbbb)
**Note : **You can write the Autocomplete Query without choosing any table also.
**Step 4 : **After filling in the required fields and giving required permission, save the Autocomplete page. ![](/cf/files/83d19871-84a3-479b-9a1c-293580538308)
**Step 5 : **The saved Autocomplete will appear in the listing page,which can be edited by clicking on the edit button. ![](/cf/files/8fd8f497-9fee-49f8-b785-5aea3fa8252a)
**Step 6 : **We can edit the Autocomplete in the Edit mode. ![](/cf/files/5083f06b-6d49-4eea-952c-0b7ef0373094)
**Step 7 : **In order to see the Demo Autocomplete, click on the Demo button. ![](/cf/files/3ecffe0c-1acb-44db-a179-bb59552d0244)
**Step 8 : **After clicking on the Demo button, this page is opened where Autocomplete default selected value is Action based on that the Autocomplete(Prefetch) is selected. ![](/cf/files/3e7c18be-2963-41b0-93fd-b5ebab0c24f7)
**Step 9 : **Then the Autocomplete(Local Storage) is selected. ![](/cf/files/22bb3522-f5ed-4609-a506-eca9172f78a6)
**Step 10 : **Then the Autocomplete(Clear Text) is selected. ![](/cf/files/6bab60b9-59ea-4b2b-9e3e-8363bc9fcfef)
**Step 11 : **A Multiselect Component can be included in this way. ![](/cf/files/912c7fd3-5de4-4d65-b0a8-c01bbb2c81fc)
**Step 12 : **A Multiselect (Dependent Multiselect) and Multiselect(Dependent Multiselect) can be included in this way. ![](/cf/files/7fe47d81-7327-40b7-943b-0a8380999ac2)
**How can I use AutoComplete for user input?**
Use the AutoComplete feature to provide real-time suggestions as users type into input fields. This helps users quickly find and select relevant options without typing the full value, improving both speed and accuracy.
Configure AutoComplete by linking it to a data source such as a predefined list or an API. As the user types, matching results are displayed dynamically, allowing selection from the suggested options. You can also customize behaviors like minimum characters to trigger suggestions and how results are displayed.
AutoComplete enhances user experience by reducing input errors, improving efficiency, and making data entry more intuitive.
**How can I use Additional Parameters in AutoComplete?**
Use Additional Parameters in AutoComplete to dynamically filter suggestion data based on another field selection or runtime value. This is mainly used in Dependent AutoComplete implementations.
Configure Additional Parameters while initializing the AutoComplete component. The parameter values can be updated dynamically and the AutoComplete can be reloaded using resetAutocomplete() to fetch filtered results.
**Example :**
let editProjectId = "${(projectData.project_id)!''}";
autocompleteCreated = $("#created_autocompleted").autocomplete({ autocompleteId: "created_autocompleted", enableClearText: true, prefetch: true, additionalParamaters: {project_id: editProjectId || ""} });
The Additional Parameter configured in the AutoComplete component can be accessed in the query using FreeMarker conditions and named parameters for dynamic data filtering.
SELECT emp_id, emp_name FROM employee WHERE (<#if project_id?? && project_id != ''> project_id = :project_id <#else> 1 = 0 <!--#if-->) AND emp_name LIKE CONCAT('%', :searchText, '%');
**How can I Fetch AutoComplete data dynamically?**
AutoComplete data can be fetched dynamically by configuring the AutoComplete component with the required autocompleteId and enabling data loading options such as prefetch. The suggestions are retrieved automatically when the user interacts with the input field.
Using prefetch : true loads the AutoComplete data in advance to improve search performance and reduce response time during user input.
**Example :**
autocompleteCreated = $("#created_autocompleted").autocomplete({ autocompleteId: "created_autocompleted", enableClearText: true, prefetch: true });
**How can I Reset AutoComplete dynamically?**
Use resetAutocomplete() to refresh the AutoComplete component and reload the suggestion data dynamically after updating runtime parameters or filtering conditions.
**Example :**
autocompleteCreated.resetAutocomplete();
**How can I use Dependent AutoComplete?**
Dependent AutoComplete is used to dynamically reload and filter an AutoComplete component based on the selected value of another AutoComplete field. This implementation enables context-based data retrieval by passing runtime parameter values to the dependent AutoComplete query.
When the parent AutoComplete value changes, the dependent AutoComplete parameter is updated dynamically and the component is refreshed using resetAutocomplete() to fetch the latest filtered dataset.
**Example :**
autocompleteCreated.options["additionalParamaters"]["project_id"] = projectId + "";
autocompleteCreated.resetAutocomplete();
**Note :** **projectId** represents the selected identifier value retrieved from the parent AutoComplete component and is passed dynamically to the dependent AutoComplete query for filtering the result set.
<u>**Additional AutoComplete/MultiSelect Functionalities :**</u>
* Use **enableClearAll** to enable clear/reset functionality in the Multiselect Autocomplete component. * Use **prefetch** to preload AutoComplete data during component initialization for improved search performance. * Use **pageSize** to configure the number of suggestion records loaded per request. * Use **render()** to customize the UI rendering of AutoComplete suggestion items. * Use **extractText()** to define which response field should be displayed in the input component. * Use **select()** to execute custom logic when a suggestion item is selected. * Use** setSelectedObject()** to programmatically assign default selected values to the AutoComplete component. * **Example :**
autocompleteCreated = $("#created_autocompleted").autocomplete({ autocompleteId: "created_autocompleted", enableClearText: true, prefetch: true });
multiselect = $("#rolesMultiselect").multiselect({ autocompleteId: "rolesAutocomplete", enableClearAll: true, });

Custom File Storage allows applications to store uploaded files in user-defined storage locations instead of default server directories. This helps manage files more efficiently and supports external storage systems such as local directories, network drives, cloud storage, or database storage.

After applying filters in Grid Utils, the grid data must be refreshed to display updated results based on the configured filter conditions. The refreshDataAndView() method reloads the grid data and updates the grid view dynamically.
manualEntries.entryName: Refresh Grid Data After Applying Filters?

manualEntries.entryContent:
Master Generator plays a pivotal role while creating any module by using JQuiver framework. It helps you to design the Listing page, Form Builder and Router from the table you have, to support your application.
<u>**How to make use of Master Generator**</u>
Use the Master Generator to quickly create and manage master data modules without writing extensive code. It allows you to define entities, fields, and configurations required for your application in a structured and automated way.
You can configure attributes such as field types, validations, and relationships, and the system will generate the corresponding components like forms, APIs, and database structures. This helps standardize development, reduce manual effort, and accelerate module creation.
Master Generator is especially useful for building reusable master data setups such as categories, configurations, or reference tables while ensuring consistency across the application.
**Step 1: **Navigate to Master Generator module from home page.
** Step 2: **Select the table from the Select Table list. This list will contain only the custom tables which are created by the user. ![](/cf/files/ef2af974-f141-4098-ad89-41d1a949aac8)
** Step 3: ** Once the table is selected, then provide the module name as per your desire. ![](/cf/files/d70873d4-1d1b-4c0d-b3fb-a724a03d76ce)
** Step 4: ** Next comes the designing of listing page. In this section, you can decide which all columns from your table should be visible on the listing page. Also it helps you to configure, if at all any column should be kept hidden and be not visible. ![](/cf/files/db318635-ff7d-4bb2-9606-a2ed3ed737e8)
** Step 5: ** When we select the custom table, then automatically the corresponding router module for listing-page is created and the router menu is enabled for the listing page. ![](/cf/files/dc82eab8-8095-44f4-a8cd-40d7ac7894f0)
** Step 6: ** Now comes the Form designing. This section will help you to design the add/edit page, for the corresponding table you have selected. Now as in listing, here also you can select the fields which should be visible, which should be hidden and which should not be considered, at all, which doing the add/update of data.For configuring Form, the corresponding router module for Add-edit page is created and the router menu is enabled for the Add-edit page. ![](/cf/files/08ca3fa5-5d51-4ec5-89e9-233c0c663326)
** Step 7: ** For enabling Captcha and CSRF validation , the corresponding Toggle Check Boxes are provided, which should be enabled or disabled as per the user requirement. ** Note:- ** CSRF validation can be done only if Captcha validation is enabled. ![](/cf/files/433a0a11-fae1-404e-82ef-a747c8b17b98)
** Step 8: ** For including File Bin in the form , the corresponding File Bin Toggle Check Box is given, upon enabling it, the file fin will be included in the form. If the user wants to include File Storage Class in File Bin, then he/she can enable the File Storage Toggle Check Box. Details about File Storage Module is given in the File Bin Module. ![](/cf/files/384e9a6a-a6dd-4315-8c60-64fc4caf35e3)
** Step 10: ** Now comes the main part. Permissions. As in all module, you can provide permission to this pages as well. Using this, you can restrict the usage of the page by the users as per their roles. ![](/cf/files/61660baf-f11b-40fb-92a1-523ea72153f2)
**Note:-** Before saving, do a cross verification on the entries and options you have made, while creating the master generator, as we have not provided edit option for this module. Once created, you will not be able to edit it.
** Output** Once this module is created, the menu will be available in the application as shown below. ![](/cf/files/976d28ad-853a-4a84-a884-2a80f90b60b1)
Logically, if we create a Master Generator, entries in 4 modules are done. **1. Template** The listing page is created through Templating Module. **2. Dynamic Form** The form is created through Form Builder. **3. Router** The menu is created through Router. **4. Grid** The Pq Grid in the listing page is created through Grid Utils.

<u>**Steps to add resource bundle key:**</u>
**Step 1: **Navigate to Internationalization.
**Step 2: **Click on Add Label.
![](/cf/files/23cd6bae-7411-48a1-a0df-6251d46e90ad)
**Step 3: **Add new label and provide translation for all languages.
![](/cf/files/af7e0224-7882-4abd-98b1-8e2cfeb0080c)
<u>**Internationalization Demo**</u>
![](/cf/files/04643fd0-0f99-48e2-9cb6-585339a10fbd)
<u>**French language**</u>
![](/cf/files/f1147460-9a8f-42da-8740-358ed18dd6b9)
<u>**Hindi language**</u>
![](/cf/files/8febc6d9-8cee-4c46-9776-96618679b4a3)
JQuiver provides few default resource bundle keys which application developer can utilize in their project like instead of JQuiver you can have your own project name in different template Just update English language content for jws.projectName and it will be reflected in following templates ![](/cf/files/ae55b1d4-78c2-403c-879d-3350b06cdca5)
**Note:** * <@resourceBundleWithDefault "jws.projectName" "JQuiver"/> * This means whatever label we add for key jws.projectName it will be reflected in that particular case where the key is used. * Else the default value is JQuiver.
![](/cf/files/f86dd4f2-8e9c-4cea-b060-17e9a90f4595)
* **Home page - home-page (Title)** ![](/cf/files/2335530b-d1f2-4dee-9600-3398a91ad499)
*** Login Page - jws-login** ![](/cf/files/bada0ed5-ea9c-4374-8cf4-352259e727af)
<u>**Import Export**</u>
In this module, Import Export provision for Resource Keys is given. There are two buttons one for Import and other for Export. ![](/cf/files/206529b7-eba9-4004-b1c3-3ca93e8c0f1b)
*When we click on Export button, Resource Keys are exported in XML format . ![](/cf/files/425f0a7c-16d2-49ab-8484-6e14bb59d677)
*Here is the sample file. ![](/cf/files/7174b451-be2d-486b-bbe1-5e27788654a9)
*When we click on Import button, an Import File dialog box opens . ![](/cf/files/2d8f96e4-5254-4741-ac6f-3f3666784f04)
*Here the corresponding file is chosen to be imported . ![](/cf/files/bf77f351-468f-4c4b-8af9-e68712589fb0)
*After importing, the Resource keys get imported in the application . ![](/cf/files/c37e67a1-aa74-4525-b42d-66a5c236b4d8)

The Import and Export functionality allows users to upload files to the server for processing and download generated or existing files from the server securely. This feature is commonly used for data migration, backups, reporting, and bulk data operations.
manualEntries.entryName: Handle Import and Export of Files in Server?

manualEntries.entryContent: The Clear Multiselect functionality allows users to remove all selected values from a multiselect dropdown quickly. This helps reset filters, clear user selections, and improve form usability without manually deselecting individual items.

<u>**Steps to add a new route.**</u>
**Step 1: **Navigate to Router module from home page.
**Step 2: **Click on Add Route button. ![](/cf/files/5e749fe5-4090-42f1-aafa-72e017d3d170)
**Step 3: **Provide all mandatory details and click on save button. **Note:** * Enter the Route Name. * Parent dropdown will be by default Root after creating Routes and after including in menu,those options would be available in the dropdown.The parent dropdown and the sequence text box will be disabled by default. Only when the Include in Menu toggle box is checked then the Parent dropdown and the sequence text box would be enabled and automatically the sequence will be populated based on the router menu. * When Open in New Tab toggle checkbox is checked then the route menu will open in a new tab. * You can style your menu by adding css class in the Menu Style textbox. * You can choose any context type from the dropdown for which you need to create the router. * The corresponding context name would be populated in Context Name autocomplete, if Template context type is choosen, then the templates would be populated in Context Name autocomplete. * You can enter your desired url in the Route Url textbox.
![](/cf/files/8a665c76-6ea7-4f82-a4e4-1b5401a601a5) ![](/cf/files/a56833f3-0ee3-42ff-8311-aef0375f89a1)
**Note: ** While adding new Route in menu make sure there is no other record exist with same Route name or with same module URL. If Include in Menu enabled then sequence number is unique in selected parent module.
You can create Route for any one of the following context type: * Dashboard * Form Builder * REST API * Root * Target URL * Template
Except for the Root as a context type, context name autocomplete will be enabled. From context name you can select any entity created from the respective context type master.
<u>**Example to include dashboard in the Router:**</u>
**Step 1:** Create new Dashboard from the dashboard master (You can refer Dashboard help manual for more information on how to create/manage dashboard) ![](/cf/files/c416c1d5-465c-49e2-96b9-7cc10459f4b2)
**Step 2:** Enter Route name and enable Include In Menu toggle button. ![](/cf/files/0b75821b-3fc6-4c90-a439-5b58cae08edf)
**Step 3:** Select Dashboard as a Context Type. When Dashboard is selected as Context Type then automatically Include Layout toggle button is enabled. ![](/cf/files/40f73b6e-c35f-45bc-86a5-683f8b60b668)
**Step 4:** Enter dashboard name that you have created in step 1 in Context Name. ![](/cf/files/8e1d8100-d202-487f-a925-b18a7b3c803d)
**Step 5:** Enter Route URL. ![](/cf/files/d5c00cf7-9caa-4102-a33c-57bdbff31901)
<u>**Example to create target Url menu in the Router:**</u>
**Step 1:** Enter Route Name, choose Root as Parent, enable Include in Menu, enable Open In New Tab if you want open the url in new tab and choose context type as Target URL automatically DELETE will be the Request Type and enter the external url which you want to open.
![](/cf/files/b0fcd312-8216-4499-a32c-3717e1d9f957)
**Step 2: ** And give the required permissions and save the route.
![](/cf/files/c9952c07-bdf6-44b0-a192-db05d8a00cf6)
**Step 3: **The router will be added in the hamburger menu, and upon clicking it, the target url will be opened in new tab.
![](/cf/files/1e7211db-74af-4baf-a390-1b08b4541c7e)
<u>**Steps to create group of routes:**</u>
**Step 1: ** Create new parent route. ![](/cf/files/ba120df8-9cd3-447b-8068-62f363d9d99f)
**Step 2: ** Create child route and select route name created in step 1 as parent route. ![](/cf/files/c1bc0488-b628-48b9-96d2-98305c726526) ![](/cf/files/4b9b0d54-f4d2-44d7-80be-a63fac673d97)
**Example:** ![](/cf/files/053515e0-21fc-457c-9a42-7049c03109ae)
**Note:** In the above example, autocomplete-demo cannot have any child node. There can be multiple child nodes from the parent i.e Templates.
<u>**Step to configure role based home page:**</u>
**Step 1: **Create new role. Navigate to User management from home page.
**Step 2: **Click on Manage Roles ![](/cf/files/c47b8aa7-3919-405a-84b7-01f572fc38d8)
**Step 3: **Click on Add Role ![](/cf/files/94393d36-1bf8-4083-9d2c-16de4de16657)
**Step 4: **Enter data in all mandatory fields and Role Priority should be unique. Click on save button ![](/cf/files/a29aac02-f32f-4753-8709-2f5f46e852cb)
**Step 5: **Once role has been created navigate to Router.
**Step 6: **Click on Define Home Page. ![](/cf/files/0eda2adf-7cf0-4dd0-95f3-77ed50af61ef)
**Step 7: **Edit newly created role ![](/cf/files/23295c6d-0bab-47fd-872a-10214f944179)
**Step 8: **Select Route Name. ![](/cf/files/2e52c447-9816-4436-9b3d-c369eba9b5b5)
**Step 9: **Click on save button ![](/cf/files/3a611f48-4953-45b5-a7e4-89d0d9357f2c)
**Step 10: **Now go back to User Management.
**Step 11: **Click on Manage Users. ![](/cf/files/535b61a7-7ade-46c0-903b-f8c3caf7c57a)
**Step 12: **Edit any user details. ![](/cf/files/d9051688-0939-493f-b822-0004858426c5)
**Step 13: **Enable newly created role and save the details ![](/cf/files/92ab2130-8770-4642-aec9-7919bb4cfa7d)
** Validations: **
* Except for Root as context type, Module URL must be unique. * If Include In Menu is enable and if Parent module is Root then Sequence must be unique at parent level or else if Parent module is other than Root then Sequence shoud be unique in that particular group.

The jq_sendError function is used to display or send error messages when an operation fails or validation errors occur in the application. It helps provide meaningful feedback to users and improves error handling within the system.

Prefetching values in Autocomplete allows the application to load suggestion data in advance before the user starts typing. This improves search performance, reduces server calls, and provides faster autocomplete suggestions.

<u>**To add a new Dynamic Form follow steps: **</u>
**Step 1: ** Open Form Builder from home page.
**Step 2: **Click on Add Form, it will open up a form to add/edit Form. ![](/cf/files/48ae2084-638c-4635-8ff0-e3566eef7076)
**Step 3:** After clicking on add , Enter form name, form description, and select Query Type (default value is Select Query) and the custom table around which form would be generated. ![](/cf/files/e06e6861-ba6c-463e-bb03-fea83ab2ca2d)
**Step 4:** The select Query Type dropdown will have the following values based on which the Select Script would be generated. ![](/cf/files/efe2818e-85a0-47c1-b4e9-161bbbceeb11)
**Note:** * If Select Query is selected from the Query type dropdown , a select script like SELECT * FROM all_datatypes would be generated in the Select Script Editor. * If Java Script/Python/ PHP is selected from the Query type dropdown ,then corresponding language specific script functions would be generated in the Select Script Editor. * The main function of the Select Script Editor is to fetch the values of that table from the database, and those values would be used in the HTML editor to populate in the input fields in the edit mode.
**Step 5:** After selecting the table , Select Script , HTML Script and Save Script would be populated. ![](/cf/files/e06e6861-ba6c-463e-bb03-fea83ab2ca2d) ![](/cf/files/d2b348d2-2917-4759-bd45-cfdda13d40bc) ![](/cf/files/0d534ed2-025d-43b4-bc30-d327b57185c6)
**Step 6:**In the Save Script Editor, the Query Type dropdown will have the following values based on which the save query can be written. ![](/cf/files/c1cbfbf9-d4d2-42aa-b016-8f78648d6702)
**Note:** * The logic written in the Save Script Editor is for saving the values entered in the form in the db table. * The Save Script logic could be written depending on the option chosen by the user from the Query Type dropdown.
**Step 7:**After entering the values, save the form with required permissions. ![](/cf/files/42bab23c-c1b6-4db6-a9e5-370fa9877dd7)
**Step 8:** All the populated fields can be altered if required by clicking on the edit button in the listing page. ![](/cf/files/5cf017d5-e9c1-4bbb-bc8c-8f0a303c4aab)
**Step 9:**The form can be edited in the edit mode and then changes can be saved further. ![](/cf/files/61c42c44-c8c4-4f91-b82a-1a6f0d3bf6b3)
**Step 10:**There is a provision for enabling Form IO by which the Form IO related form would be generated. ![](/cf/files/d94b3518-d835-4265-8699-aa3abc2583c3)
**Note:**For Form IO the select Query Type value by default will be Java Script and the same table which was used in Form IO should be used to generate the Form for Form IO. ![](/cf/files/1b9ecc49-2ca8-4fa0-a686-b9aa0990c888)
**Step 11:** After selecting the table , Select Script , HTML Script and Save Script for Form IO would be populated. ![](/cf/files/6ed4d9dd-b837-46d8-a03a-3720432861b9) ![](/cf/files/3b358b1b-bc49-4ef9-ae75-dfc9f6452dbe) ![](/cf/files/27f8447b-4e43-4715-ae48-e3b972c05942)
**Note:** For Form IO, detailed description is given in the Help Manual of Form IO.
**Note:** There is a provision for adding Script Library in Form Builder, detailed description of which is given in the Script Library Help Manual.

Importing the configuration plays pivotal role when you want to migrate some configuration or data from an existing database to another database.
To import a configuration browse and upload the particular zip file already exported and saved in the local drive. Once uploaded, all the configuration to be imported will be listed down with the existing version number and import version number. Existing version number is the number which is available in your current setup, if the configuration already exists and import version number will be the number of the configuration from the setup from which it was exported. The configuration can imported all together or can be be imported one by one after review, by the user. This can also be compared, with the existing version in the system, if any, using the comparison icon (if enabled).
<u>**Step 1: ** Go to Home page and click on the Import Button.</u>
![](/cf/files/7a1e3f09-d3dd-4e39-bdf2-3d5003e551be)
**Step 2: ** Import Configuration page is opened, where the zip file is selected for import.
![](/cf/files/8a277a0a-aa06-45db-aa3d-44a8cbbafa25)
**Step 3: ** By clicking on the import button the files from the zip gets listed in the grid.
![](/cf/files/8f8c8daf-8538-4168-ad71-e31ac523d8ef)
By clicking on the download button, the individual file gets imported and by clicking on the Import All button all the files in the list gets imported.
**Step 4: ** Comparison of the imported configuration is done by clicking on the comparison button and can be viewed as in versioning module.
![](/cf/files/ae754fd5-89ac-48a1-8248-5b62aba77384)
**Step 5: ** After the comparison is done, the changes are copied and then saved successfully.

A REST API (Representational State Transfer Application Programming Interface) is a web service interface that allows systems to communicate over the internet using standard HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE.
REST APIs follow a stateless, client-server architecture, making them simple, scalable, and easy to integrate with different applications.
Rest API lets you write API's in below languages : 1. FTL (Freemaker) 2. Java 3. Javascript 4. Python 5. PHP
<u>**Follow the steps below to create a REST API dynamically.**</u>
**Step 1: **Visit the REST API module on Jquiver home.
**Step 2 : **Click on Add REST API, it will open up a form to add/edit REST API ![](/cf/files/918a607b-753c-43d8-955c-383b70629fae) ![](/cf/files/570e9aad-b89b-4a03-b08b-07df318e99db) ![](/cf/files/1904c6f2-9e2f-401a-92ef-24f56e8698e2) ![](/cf/files/4738696e-a45d-436d-b9fc-8d0d582444ef)
**Available HTTP Method Type: **
* POST * GET * PATCH * PUT * DELETE * OPTIONS * HEAD * TRACE
**Available HTTP Produce Type: **
* application/json * application/octet-stream * application/xml * image * text/html
**Available Platform Type: **
* FTL * Java * JavaScript * Python * PHP
**Type of queries: **
* Select Query * Insert-Update-Delete Query * Stored Procedure * REST Client
<u>**Steps to create new REST API for FTL:**</u>
**Step 1: ** Provide unique REST URL ![](/cf/files/566052ac-cf76-49ce-b213-dfc384f74923)
**Step 2: ** Select appropriate HTTP Method Type ![](/cf/files/78a189bb-a01c-4916-89c5-4f7b97d39a58)
**Step 3: ** Select appropriate HTTP Produces Type ![](/cf/files/c19d68cd-83ba-4888-928e-4518c3481b7c)
**Step 4: ** Select FTL as platfrom type and write service logic using FTL directives, expression etc. ![](/cf/files/13c874e0-0704-47f8-96a2-67a16b7df3f0)
**Service logic is written as shown below:**
![](/cf/files/0abe0a6d-a11f-428f-92e2-0c5b1219569b)
**Step 5: ** You can write any type of query as shown in the image below. There are no restriction on number of queries. ![](/cf/files/0e132c56-a984-45da-b2d0-f84982970a02)
**Example to call and execute FTL REST API in any template: ** ![](/cf/files/bfbf0453-5edf-496a-bbec-dee959a34ef7)
<u>**Steps to create new REST API for Java:**</u>
**Step 1: ** Provide unique REST URL ![](/cf/files/d9b6dd66-0f0a-40e6-8cef-15c6efa861f3)
**Step 2: ** Select appropriate HTTP Method Type ![](/cf/files/79e37ea0-6b50-45fd-97a2-dde0937b0b51)
**Step 3: ** Select appropriate HTTP Produces Type ![](/cf/files/26d47021-c664-4d5f-aab9-89c5a0281a08)
**Step 4: ** Enter unique method name and that method with Method Signature must be present in Java file provided in service logic ![](/cf/files/57a80913-7368-4797-a46d-248797499a4d)
**Step 5: ** Select Java as platfrom type. You can have your own business logic in selected method. In the service logic editor enter the class name with package as shown in the image below. ![](/cf/files/79f916c5-3d09-4443-ae5e-871ef468e1b1)
**Step 6: ** You can write any type of query as shown in the image below. There are no restriction on number of queries. ![](/cf/files/92f68954-937a-4f66-b1b2-c0edf78c3d31) ![](/cf/files/1144b319-c78f-48bf-b3ee-d39d7706ff08) ![](/cf/files/f2e94f83-fe1b-4c10-accb-585c6dc78a5c)
**Example to call and execute Java REST API in any template: ** ![](/cf/files/87ed30d9-203f-45e2-b28a-3d2fe1393225)
<u>**Steps to create new REST API for JavaScript:**</u>
**Step 1: ** Provide unique REST URL ![](/cf/files/a98f62e8-7edf-497a-9f7e-d1ea80412e4c)
**Step 2: ** Select appropriate HTTP Method Type ![](/cf/files/cf5967b0-e7a9-4617-9796-5d09c618101f)
**Step 3: ** Select appropriate HTTP Produces Type ![](/cf/files/7de7f045-464c-4833-81be-31a156e362ff)
**Step 4: ** Select JavaScript as platfrom type. ![](/cf/files/863e01cc-d650-4b38-8aae-5d9fd77ee9a9)
Write service logic using JavaScript variable, functions, objects etc. as shown in the image below. Make sure to call your function after its declaration. ![](/cf/files/7ae6c1e4-b19e-462c-992a-5da74fa8d96c)
**Step 5: ** You can write any type of query. There are no restriction on number of queries. ![](/cf/files/86cfa8fe-54d3-449e-baf0-2b9e7958fd62)

Exporting the configuration plays pivotal role when you want to migrate some configuration or data from an existing database to another database.
**Step 1: ** Go to Home page and click on the Export Button.
![](/cf/files/f068fa05-6f66-4272-aeb1-f206bbee7bef)
**Step 2: ** In the Export Module Page, by default all Custom Modules are displayed and all modules are selected and system configurations deselected.
![](/cf/files/a7097cba-a7c5-4f69-a514-30336afe5e19)
**Step 3: ** By checking the check box of Deselect button , all the modules are deselected.If at all user wants to reset the default selection and export only particular configuration, then that can be achieved by "Deselect All" checkbox.
![](/cf/files/8ba2ceea-21fb-4810-a6f3-c546cabf6f50)
**Step 4: ** Custom option can be also changed from the Show drop-down.
![](/cf/files/9189c80b-0151-4d87-9eed-0911ff2c6694)
**Step 5: ** To export a configuration go to particular module on the export configuration page and select the same.By checking the Permission check box, the permission for the same modules can also be exported in the same zip.
![](/cf/files/0348a0e1-ac68-49eb-a0f5-db241e512028)
**Step 6: ** Once the configuration to be exported are finalized, go to the preview page using "Next" button and get it exported in zip format.
![](/cf/files/f870d845-5f40-4ee0-b20e-585eea560045)
**Step 7: ** By clicking on the Export Zip button, the files are exported in the zip format.
![](/cf/files/13685dc4-5866-4f91-a927-95e28b39c3e3)

File Bins play a pivotal role in managing the files using JQuiver application. We as a JQuiver application helps you to manage your files between different users and roles as per your requirement. Create File Bins for the application for uploading files. Users can include file bin in any form, template or module and upload multiple files of any format, size as per their requirements. There is a provision for Custom File Storage also.
**How can I use File Bin to manage files?**
Use the File Bin to store, organize, and manage uploaded files within the application. It acts as a centralized repository where you can upload, view, download, and delete files as needed.
You can categorize files, search for specific documents, and reuse existing files across different modules without uploading them again. The File Bin also helps in maintaining file versions and ensuring proper organization of documents.
Using File Bin improves file management efficiency, reduces duplication, and provides easy access to all uploaded resources in one place.
<u>**To add a new File Bin follow steps: ** </u>
**Step 1: ** Open File Bins from home page.
**Step 2: **Click on Add File Bin, it will open up a form to add/edit File Bin. ![](/cf/files/d3580ccd-8f4e-4ca1-b824-03fe6acad44f)
**Step 3:** After clicking on add , Enter File Bin Id, File Type Supported,Max File Size, No of files etc. ![](/cf/files/c70f4b62-d920-4624-a31b-b818eb7f1544)
**For a File Bin there are different properties to be specified.**
| Property Name | Usage | | -------- | -------- | | File-bin-id | Unique id, used to identify the file bins. This ids cannot be duplicate and it helps you to manage the files. | | File types supported |Different types of file to be supported in that file bin. Providing specific extensions, helps you to add restrictions on the upload of file. If no restrictions has to be made, then you can add * | | Max file size | This is the size of file which is to be uploaded. JQuiver has provided the conversion of file sizes between, bytes, kilo bytes and mega bytes. As per users understanding and comfortability, size can be provided. | | No of files | This will help to manage and provide a restriction for the user on file upload count. |
There is a provision for providing Custom File Storage Class, for which the File Storage toggle button should be enabled.
Apart from the above properties, user can provide specific restrictions on Upload, View and Delete files in a file bin. To provide these restrictions, JQuiver has provided specific toggle buttons for all the three features.
Initially the Upload Validator, View Validator and Delete Validator toggle buttons would be disabled which means, no restrictions will be there for any of the operations on the files uploaded in that particular file bin. Depending on the requirements , the toggle buttons would be enabled. ![](/cf/files/3043e1da-ace2-41e0-80a9-c00bfc5e4f18)
If user wants to provide restriction on any of the functionality or all of the feature, then the toggle buttons would be enabled and query has to be written as per users requirement.
We can provide restrictions for uploading a file. By default there wont be any restrictions. If we turn on this toggle button, then a default query will be generated. You can update the query as per your requirement. Depending on the Query Type selected, the content of the Monaco editor would be changed. ![](/cf/files/88434124-6d3a-4330-8c57-1bda0e623e7f)
**For Javascript:** ![](/cf/files/0c5893cc-8fe2-478c-8262-e11399c73e3b)
**For Python:** ![](/cf/files/38722876-59a2-4f23-beca-6812444a2aaf)
**For PHP:** ![](/cf/files/843ab443-b45b-457b-9eb5-b539af54bf87)
The query should return data with alias "isAllowed". If value of isAllowed is 1, then upload will be allowed. If it is 0, then upload will be restricted.
<u>**View**</u> As explained above, We can provide restrictions for downloading an uploaded file as well. By default there wont be any restrictions. If we enable the toggle button then the query will be generated. You can update the query as per your requirement. The query should return data with alias "isAllowed". If value of isAllowed is 1, then view/download will be allowed. If it is 0, then view/download will be restricted. ![](/cf/files/fe297477-dd0a-4647-8844-dc65c2356d3b)
<u>**Delete**</u> Using this, restriction criteria can be provided for deleting a file. By default there wont be any restrictions. If we enable the toggle button, then a default query will be generated. You can update the query as per your requirement. The query should return data with alias "isAllowed". If value of isAllowed is 1, then delete will be allowed. If it is 0, then delete will be restricted. ![](/cf/files/39cbb1c3-7472-4e12-9748-6400bf7a2fec)
In the above example of delete query, restriction is provided on deleting files as per owner. That is, user can delete files, uploaded by them. They wont have rights to delete files uploaded by any other user. Whereas, Admin will have right to delete all the files, irrespective of the file owner.
**By default JQuiver has provided four File Bins as shown in the image below.** ![](/cf/files/bf72927a-0a50-466b-b284-0854bc012e85)
1.**default :-** Default usage. All the Files will be uploaded on the Common Files. 2.** helpManual :- **This file bins manages the files specifically for JQuiver Help Manuals 3.**profilePic:- **This file bin is used for uploading the profile pic of the user. 4.**mailAttachment:-** This file bin is used for attaching files in the mail.
**For uploading files, in the Common Files section click on the Common Files icon as shown in the picture:** ![](/cf/files/28e77767-8fad-4d46-a126-666413aed318)
In this section, as is shown in the picture below you can upload files if you are an Admin, Anonymous user wont be able to upload files. Here default file bin is used. ![](/cf/files/a2da1bf7-67cf-4ec0-96ef-d89604f61003) ![](/cf/files/f1968fb9-559b-47fd-bdc7-07e6b3453263)
There is also a provision of adding Script Library in the File Bins. For creating a Script Library you need to click on the Script Library icon as shown in the image below: ![](/cf/files/69b75a97-fe0b-460e-ab0d-116821a30727)
* For including Script Library in File Bins, there is a button provided upon each validators which is initially disabled. * Script Library button would be available only for Javascript, Python and PHP. ![](/cf/files/aeda35e3-bd33-4485-bba8-c43c35693f51)
Details about Script Library is provided in the Script Library Help Manual.

**Step 2 : **Click on Add Property, it will open up a form to add/edit Property. ![](/cf/files/f572d5d4-61ab-4dbd-9b87-1b73b24041c2)
